title: Good for dry hair, not good for wet
text: I have split opinions on this flat iron. If you are ironing dry hair and like a wider plate (this one is 1.25”), it does a nice job straightening. However, as far as working on wet hair, it is terrible. Both my college-aged daughter and I both tried it on wet hair and both of us came to the same conclusion. While the iron did dry my wet hair, it also caused it to be frizzy, for lack of a better word. After using it, my hair was not smooth and shiny like it is after I iron my already blown dry hair. Instead my hair looked “damaged” and rough around the edges. I have naturally wavy hair that is thick. Perhaps this would work better on someone with thinner, straighter hair. I use a flat iron after every time I blow dry my hair and thought this would be a good shortcut. Nope. I’m sticking with my regular process of blowing dry first and ironing afterward. I will likely keep this iron as my back-up since it does a perfectly fine job once my hair is already dry.

title: Wonderful to Use as a Shave Oil, No More Itchy Legs or Underarms :-)
text:
I know this is a baby bath oil, but I'm a fan of using bath oils as shave oils. A little goes a long way, just a few drops per leg (or you can add a drop to your regular shave gel). Helps reduce irritation and you can see where you are shaving! I can't live without it. It's better than any foam, gel or lotion on the market. Truly the best shave experience. I have thick hair and dry flaky skin that is prone to itching and sensitivity. I use this bath oil with a cheapie disposable razor and a few drops of body oil after a shower and my legs are silky for days. Literally days. No razor burn, no irritation, no frustration. I prefer unscented products and this is truly unscented--thank you! Please use caution when using oils in the tub, but I haven't had any problems with the tub getting slippery. Only thing I would change in a better bottle with a pump or dropper top. It's easy to use too much. A little goes a long way so if you're hesitant about the price, don't be, because you'll end up spending more in the long run on something cheaper.

Also, used this as a body wash after getting a little too much sun today, and my skin was super soothed. I think it will help prevent my skin from developing a sunburn--bonus! I may just have to buy a vat of this!

Hope you found this review helpful!

title: Smooth and scentless
text:
I am very happy with this oil. This is the first time I've tried a fractionated coconut oil rather than regular cold pressed, and I was very surprised and happy with the differences between the two.

This fractionated coconut oil is liquid at all times, which is the purpose of it of course, but it has a wonderful feel to it that is not at all what you would expect after using a regular coconut oil. It absorbs quite well into the skin and doesn't leave behind any sticky or slimy residue.

There is no discernable odor to this oil at all, so using it as a carrier oil for essential oil blends or anything with added fragrance will not be polluted by an undesirable scent.

I love that the bottle come with both a regular bottle top or pump cap. This makes it easy to chose which style will best suit your needs.

I would definitely recommend this oil to someone looking for an unscented carrier oil.

title: Good product but quantity misleading: Only one pen included!
text:
Is it just me? I have purchased this exact product several times--happily--in dark brown, grey brown, and chestnut (red brown). My eyebrows are, like my natural hair, about a level 4 or 5 dark brown, and the grey brown is probably the closest, though the other colors are handy for changes of color to hair and makeup. My brows are thinning, but a little filling in does the trick, and these pens are my favorite tool for the job, as they both color in where you need solid filling and can be used to make feathery strokes for a more natural brow look. This pen came with a useless little razor and three eyebrow "templates," only one of which I might consider useful (if you are new to shaping your brows).
The problem:
In the past I have received (I see now from other sellers) at least two or three pens for $6.99 to $8.99. I can't believe I paid over $8 for just one pen from this seller "Generies." Going back over the seller page, I see that indeed, by the pictures it appears that one will receive two pens. A sneaky little trick, showing the same photos as the sellers with better deals, but neglecting to include the "2 CT." If you are busy or distracted in the least, you might fall for it, as did I--and doubtless, as have many customers.
I really dislike writing negative comments, but am doing this to save others the trouble and disappointment of feeling ripped off. Needless to say, I will be buying this product from other sellers in future--the ones with the more reasonable--and honest--deals.

title: Still a classic quality Norelco trimmer.
text:
I used to have an old Norelco trimmer that used for years until it just wouldn't charge anymore and bought this as the "new and improved" replacement and for the most part . . . it is.
Here is the short:

Pros: Classic Design, Same quality and function, powerful vacum
Cons: Not good for trimming to skin w/o Guard.

The Long:

Its form, design and cut remind me of my old Norelco (which is great) with only one major difference, the vacuum. In order for the vacum to work there is a plastic vacum "sleeve" or "guard" that goes up to the teeth. The other reviewers complained about close trimming without the full guard and I do agree that it is awkward to pull down the vacuum sleeve for a close trim but I found that on both this model and my old one, the teeth on the trimmer were too big for a comfortable close trim anyway, so I do not use that feature. I instead use a fine tooth slid-up trimmer on another shaver for that. I disagree with the noise complaints, I thought the vacuum was only slightly louder than the actual trimmer and there was plenty of suction at the lower setting, which you can't hear over the trimmer. Overall, a great classic Norelco trimmer.

text: I bought some shine spray from a no-return retailer [that'll teach me!] and when I got it home the sprayer did not work no matter what I did to try to unplug it. The jade stones; they’re bigger than I thought, which I love. It has a nice cool temperature & I change the glue out about ever 30 mins or so. I do put tape down on the jade stone so I don’t ruin it.. I got glue on the stone itself and it didn’t come off which is why I recommend tape.

title: Product pictures should show the item you actually receive
text:
The good news is the wig is thick and the quality is as good as your standard decent amazon costume wig. The bad news is it arrives looking nothing like any of the photos on Amazon. It's really not okay to sell an item with pictures that look nothing like the actual item. Sure, show it styled, but have at least one picture of the item people will receive, and make it clear styling is required. And if you really care about your customers, provide some instructions for styling it the way it is shown.

Also, gave less stars because the color is different in reality than in the pictures. The blue is more cornflower than cobalt, without the variegation that seems to be in the product pictures.
(Sadly missed the return window due to traveling)

title: Great for an electric, but still doesn't compare to fusion razor
text:
I gave this to my husband, hoping he would get hooked by even the novelty of it and use it everyday, I like a freshly clean shaven look on him and he had been slacking lately using his fusion disposables.

He's tried a few electric razors in the past but was never happy with them so has stuck to using the upper end disposables.

His take: very comfortable to hold, the heads conform to his face well and it's a comfortable shave... as long as it's very lubricated. He found he got the best feeling and closest share only when he used a lot of shaving creme/gel & had his face very well, making for quite a mess on the sink top. He liked the idea of the dispensing lotion but found he preferred his normal shaving gel and also didn't think the dispenser held enough or that it was cost effective. On the upside this is water proof so I'll get him to try it in the shower next. He also agrees with the other reviewers who said it's just not a close enough shave, and that it takes far to long. The whole idea of a electric is to speed up the process but between loading it with the conditioner, using it, adding more water, cleaning up the mess etc it was taking him 2x as long to shave at least, and with less steller results.

My take: I think his face looks better, the shave may not be as close however there are no knicks or aggrivation and his skin looks better all around. Of course I see his point on the time consuming aspect.

The booklet recommends giving it 3 weeks, I'm not sure I'll be able to talk him into that... wonder how this thing would work on my legs? ;)

title: In order to get it in my hair without looking like a mess (my hair is thin
text:
This is my first time using bun donuts. In order to get it in my hair without looking like a mess (my hair is thin, but I have a lot of it and my ponytail is long), I made a tight ponytail, brought the donut to the end and wrapped the end of my ponytail over it. Then I rolled down over itself. It held the bun evenly and tightly without bobby pins but I added four pins for my own security. I have had it in for two days and it didn't budge, even with sleeping on it! I am using the medium size in the picture. I have received many compliments and I am extremely pleased with the outcome!

My four stars is because there are very little instructions. I struggled when I followed what was on the box. After a bunch of attempts, I used the method I previously discussed and it was good.

text: I'm an experienced at-home peeler, and have used many Jessner's products sold by others on both Amazon and other web pages.  This is without quesiton the weakest Jessner's I have ever used (and techincally, they are all, by definition, suppossed to have the same relative concentration of the active ingredients).  I got no results, at all, period.  I kept putting on layers, thinking that my face was just used to it (last time I did a Jessner's was over a month ago, so there should have been some reaction).  But I got nothing.  No tingling, no redness, no anything, nothing.  In addition, the consistency of the solution is very odd.  It's similar to putting a sugar syrup on your face-  far more viscous than any other Jessner's I have ever used.  Something is wrong with this product.  If this is your first time with a home Jessner's peel, I would recommend going elsewhere.

The tint darkened my brows and made them appear thicker while also looking completely natural.  By dying the lighter-colored hairs above my brow, it also seemed to raise my arches.  The only downside to the product is that it will temporarily dye the skin underneath your brow which isn't a flattering look, but I was able to remove the staining with [[ASIN:B00DL77W0A Almay Oil Free Gentle Eye Makeup Remover Pads 120 Ct]].

title: Inexpensive, works great, but completely died in 10 months (2 Units)
text: I purchased two of these together on a deep discount (half price or something). Kept one at home and one in my car for backup, shaving during commuting or at work. I mixed use between the two and a bic safety razor pretty regularly. The two electric shavers both stopped working within a few days of each other during month 10. They appeared to be fully charged but the motor became very slow and then just didn't start up again. A few times I heard a quick buzz sounding like the motor was trying but couldn't start. I think these are probably brushed motors and the brushes just failed. I took one apart and the battery was fine (just a normal Lithium cell). In the end, it's not that bad of a value - $10 a pop and lasts almost a year. Costs less than the replacement blade head on most name brand electric shavers and less than 3 or 4 disposable safety razors blade sets from the name brands. But I hate filling the landfill, so that's a bummer.

title: The smell isn’t bad, but it is not good either
text:
This is a very small amount of mud. The inside dimensions of the bottle are approximately 1.5” tall by less than 2” diameter. When I opened the bottle and removed the safety seal, the mud was to the top of the bottle. The directions include a step to stir before applying the mud. When I stirred it, I found there was a large air bubble and the bottle was only about half full. A little of this mud goes a long way, so this small amount will go further than some other facial masks.

As per the instructions, I washed and dried my face before application. The AntiAging MegaMud goes on very smoothly. It has a strong minty smell that becomes less overwhelming with time and is partially replaced with other smells that aren’t entirely pleasant. The smell isn’t bad, but it is not good either.

The mud made my skin sting immediately, but it became less intense until I washed off the dried mask. I had removed the mask and I felt my face burning still about 5-10 minutes later, so I washed my face again using cleanser. I was fortunate that there was only the burning sensation and not a significant amount of reddening.

The box says the peptides relax muscle tension by inhibiting the release of neurotransmitters released by the brain. I'm not sure how I feel about having a topical beauty product interfering with my neurotransmitters. I'm probably getting new lines as my brow furrows trying to wrap my brain around this concept.

From the company website (https://www.viacosmedics.com/product/megamud/), this is a list of Ingredients: Deionized Water, Kaolin, Propylene Glycol, Montmorillonite (Bentonite), Glycerin USP, Magnesium Aluminum Silicate, Butylene Glycol, Carbomer, Polysorbate-20, Palmitoyl Oligopeptide, Palmitoyl Tetrapeptide-7, Acetyl Hexapeptide-8, Sodium Hyaluronate, Cucumis Sativus (Cucumber) Extract, Camelia Sinensis (Green Tea) Extract, Matricaria Recutita (Chamomile) Flower Extract, Symphytum Officinale (Comfrey) Extract, Ananas Sativus (Pineapple) Extract, Cucurbita Pepo (Pumpkin) Extract, Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Juice, Alpha Lipoic Acid, Algae Extract, Punica Granatum (Pomegranate) Extract, Calendula Officinalis (Marigold) Flower Extract, Argania Spinosa (Argan) Kernel Oil, Squalene, Eucalyptus Globulus (Eucalyptus) Leaf Oil, Phenoxyethanol, Caprylyl Glycol, Potassium Sorbate, Hexylene Glycol

From the other reviews, I thought I would like this product. After a certain age, many of us probably hope for that product that really will combat wrinkles and give us just a little more youthful appearance. I can’t imagine spending over 20 dollars for such a tiny amount of product that makes my skin burn and may or may not have anti-aging properties. For now, I’ll just have to settle for having my beauty come from within. Not such a bad thing anyway.

The good news is that this product was provided at a discounted price in exchange for my honest review.

title: Looks great - even on short nails!
text:
These nail polish appliques look great - even on short nails! I don't know if the pictures actually do them justice, but they're very sparkly and look like 'real' nail polish.

They're extremely easy to apply but are very stretchy and can tear easily, if you don't take your time. Just be careful when you apply them. Once in place, I smoothed the polish strip down with my thumb and then gently slid a soft tipped orange stick over it to make sure that all the air bubbles were out and that the polish strip was securely in place. The package also includes a mini nail file so that you can file off the excess and get into the corners of your nails, which may be hard to do with a larger nail file. The instructions on how to apply them were clear and it took me about 10-15 minutes to put them on.

It's been about a week and they still look great!

title: 360° SLIM Version (blue box) worth it for small dogs!!
text:
Overall: The Company that makes these toothbrushes are a God-send, FOR REAL. Please don't stop manufacturing these!!

Review for: 360° DOG TOOTHBRUSH SLIM VERSION (Blue Box)
Use: 11lb. Senior Age Rat Terrier Chihuahua with highly sensitive gums and very yellow teeth.

My husband and I have searched high and low for 360 toothbrush to use on our little puppo since buying one while we were on our trip to Japan and was SO SO HAPPY to find this on Amazon! Our puppo's gums bleed very easily, she hates getting her teeth brushed and we needed a toothbrush that 1. Had narrower cylinder to brush her teeth more effectively and 2. Was soft and wouldn't irritate her gums.

This 360° SLIM TOOTHBRUSH brush, worked well. She doesn't mind it as badly as when we used the other competing toothbrushes but it was definitely a little bit more firm than the YELLOW SOFT TOOTHBRUSH made by this same company. title: curable peel off base coat that lasts
text:
I love gel nails but hate the soak off removal process. I learned about peel off base coats but most of them weren’t curable under a UV/LED lamp and I didn’t want to wait for air drying.

This one cures under a lamp and lets me peel off my gel manicure easily.

Some TIPS on getting it to last: Definitely buff your nails so there is no more shine on them. Buff the sides and tips well too. Then swipe them with alcohol or acetone to get them bone dry. Then use the thinnest layer possible of the Murron gel peel off base coat. Cure for 30 seconds or more. Apply your gel color and top coat as normal.

With this method my nails are lasting like normal gel and have going strong for a week now without chipping or lifting. I peeled one off just to make sure it was still easily peelable and it successfully peeled without damage to my nail.

A+ product.

title: Quick and easy mask- no sticky residue
text:
I have sensitive, acne-prone skin, and this time of year it always stays on the dry side, so I’ll take any moisturizing treatment I can get.

I used this mask this morning before applying makeup and I was really impressed. Usually I’ll use masks at night and wash my face in the morning, they tend to leave a slight unpleasant residue (IMO) so I would never put makeup directly over them. In the case of these masks there was no unpleasant feeling and I felt comfortable applying my makeup directly about 15 minutes later. It moisturized well and overall felt cooling and nice. I can’t speak to any long-term benefits yet but these seem great for a morning routine if you have 10-20 extra minutes to spare.

Just a note— mine leaked a bit in shipping. I recommend not shipping these with anything that may be damaged by a small amount of liquid.

title: No smell
text: So I've been looking forward to trying these since I got them but its been way too hot to take a bath. I assumed they would release so many good smells that I waited until my boyfriend and I could take a bath together. First the instructions aren't all that helpful. I've never used a bath bomb before but I wasn't sure if I was supposed to completely unwrap it or not. Now I realize this seems obvious but when you get the bomb in your hand its really hard to tell. So we ended up taking off the outer paper wrapping and the styrofoam wrapping. We stuck in a tub full of hot water and waited. We saw the bomb dissolving and it released a ton of pieces of what I assumed were the herbs they used but no smell. So we assumed we'd smell it when we got in. There was no smell and tons of pieces of whatever in the tub. It didn't make for a very romantic bath at all. I have eucalyptus oil which works in the bath so much better than these. Overall I'd say it isn't worth the price. The only reason I'm ok with these is I got them for free.

title: To date the fastest, most convenient, least messy shave I've had
text:
This shaver really is a treat to use. I have had no difficulty with the "grade" of shave slipping mid shave and I find it a very comfortable shaver to use.
The shave is quick and the receptacle really does seem to catch upwards of 95% of what I'm using.
So far I've had four shaves off the one charge and I shave once a week, so the battery seems to hold its charge really well.
Cleaning is a cinch.
It's noisy with turbo on, but certainly not in the same vicinity as a hair dryer. It's certainly not anything that's a detraction from the shave.
The only reason I haven't given this 5 stars is because of the area just under the nose, the other reviewers that have mentioned this are correct - it's very hard to get to this area without contorting your nose and/or lip.
I simply remove the protector and do the last few hairs freehand with the open shaver or a pair of cuticle scissors.
The overall performance of this shaver more than makes up for this one shortcoming.
Very nice carrying bag too included.

title: Good idea but poorly executed vials
text:
The little vials of the whitening agent are very poorly designed - I had one that was leaking, and one that the brush portion was stuck in the lid so it couldn't be used (there was just a little "pipe" in the "goo" part). The remaining ones worked but it's very hard to get all of the "goo" out so you end up wasting it - think of a toothpaste tube which was solid plastic and couldn't be squeezed! Once you do get it to come out, the "brush" really just makes the stuff clump and glob up - it doesn't just brush on like you'd expect.

Once you get the chemical out and use the system it works well, but I've lost so much of the chemical to bad packaging that now I need to order another set of vials to finish the treatment. It's not cheap, unfortunately, so I hope that they come up with a better packaging method for future purchasers so there isn't so much wasted whitening agent.

title: AMAZING PRODUCT but scent too strong and only found in small bottle
text:
I have thick curly coily AA hair.

It smells a tad too strong and perfumy, and does remind me of waterfalls and springs. It is thicker and creamier than other Suave naturals. It provides great slip, makes my hair soft and fluffy hours after drying, and my hair curls instantly, which lets me know my hair is moisturized.

Waterfall Mist is a great conditioner but not the best Suave bargain. You can find it in stores for as low as $0.85-$1, but it's only available in 12 ounces, whereas Suave Coconut is $1.50 for 33 ounces. So in the long run, this particular scent is not the best purchase for your money. My favorite Suave is Everlasting Sunshine and Coconut, which work even more amazingly on my hair but come in larger sizing with more pleasant scents. For this reason I would not repurchase this particular scent again even though I had a great experience with it. If you don't mind paying the same price for less product and really like the scent, then this is a great buy for you.

title: Getting Apple CarPlay actually is just as simple as popping out the old and inserting the new!
text: I have a 2016 F-150 XLT with Sync 3 and the large 8" touchscreen. I've been using android phones and the android auto feature since I bought the truck. I just recently switched back to iPhone and after doing some research found that all you need to do to get CarPlay is switch out the USB module. I've seen these for as little as $40 but most on Amazon are about $60. Installation was simple. I just used a thin flathead screwdriver, popped out one end and used my keys to hold it open (if you don't put something in to hold the one side it will pop back in when you go to open the other side). Swapping out the connections was easy. Snapped it back in place, started up the truck, a new screen came on asking to tap ok for CarPlay and voila! I now have Apple CarPlay and it works great!.

I assume the skinniest tube is also for a curling iron? or maybe a hair brush handle? Bottom line - measure your hair care tools before ordering. Suggest you advertise it with tools inserted.

title: DRYING - Not for actually dry skin
text:
Firstly, this cushion is supposed to be for dry skin, but performs very poorly on skin that's actually dry. It emphasized existing dryness and it's overly matte finish made dry spots appear that weren't initially there. Separated badly around my nose - which I don't normally have a problem with. Shade selection is abysmal (2 shades, really?). Also it's ridiculously expensive for what you get. Normally cushions at this price point come with a cushion refill. This one does not. It is only 15g or .53 oz - the standard foundation size is 1 oz. Effectively, you could get an 80 dollar foundation for the same value. Fortunately you don't even have to do that. There are many drugstore foundations that outperform this one for me.
Granted, I'm just one person. If you have normal skin that doesn't produce any oil whatsoever throughout the day, you might enjoy this.

title: Roll on Wax Bar
text:
The wax warmer is very color pleasing and so are the bars of wax that go inside the warmer itself. It comes with two roll on wax bars, along with finishing wipes (just oil towel) and a pack of paper. The directions are very easy to read on how to use the wax warmer but the pack of papers is awful in the direction department. So many spelling/grammar errors - but the pictures are more explanatory then the actual words themselves.

It took about 5-8 minutes for the wax to melt and be at a good use of temperature. I would wait until MAX temperature and then wait a few more minutes for the wax to go on smoother. Then slap the paper on.

Now, you are wondering why 3 stars?

1. The time it takes to warm up - 1 Star
2. The materials that are given plus directions - 2 Star

If you are doing a spot on shave sure go ahead but to do your entire leg and not to mention BOTH of them, you will be sitting for a very long time. Its too time consuming and I am impatience. I would suggest to buy more papers if you are thinking about getting this. I do feel that it is a learning curve and that is ok, I will continue to learn how to do it.

It does work though, works decently well once the wax is at the perfect temperature. Oh its not painless, but it is tolerable if you have a high pain tolerance, but if you DO NOT, I would suggest waxing to not be your thing.

title: Disappointing - Razor IS NOT Washable As Depicted & Cutter Block Got Dull Very Quickly
text:
I wanted a USB-chargeable pocket razor to use on my face while in the hospital. I especially liked that the WIOR Razor was listed as washable, and that the photos in the listing even included one showing a hand holding the razor under a stream of water.

The razor is nicely boxed, but I noted the unit itself was quite light compared to other travel razors about the same size I've used in recent years (my favorite one is no longer available in America, alas). Still, a lighter razor is no big deal, as long as it works.

I read the instruction leaflet - only to immediately see a warning not to expose the razor to water! ( ¿ ?) I double checked the Amazon listing to make sure I was sent the right razor, and I certainly was - however, the depiction of it as washable - particularly the image of someone holding it under a faucet stream - is highly misleading. One must remove the foil screen frame and then pull of the cutter block (while being careful the tiny spring does not fall out), and then wash the block separately.

I quickly phoned Amazon's product support number, to explain the major discrepancy, as was told measures would bet taken to correct the listing so no one else would be mislead. I had the option of returning the razor, but given my immediate need, decided to keep it.

It worked reasonably well the first week or so, but the cutter block quickly dulled and shaving became a bit painful over the course of the next couple weeks. I certainly did carefully wash the cutter block by removing the foil in its frame, and then pulling off the block (and each time the little spring would pop off, but fortunately was never lost). However, by last week, just 32 days after purchase, the shaver was cutting quite poorly - even causing a bit of pain to use. In only a month, with light use, the cutting block had dulled to the point of uselessness.

I must point out that despite my call about the misleading product description and imagery, the washability feature remains in the listing as of this writing, five weeks later.

I am very disappointed to see that no action was taken, despite the assurances I was given back in April.

title: Great polish and great deal! Even you get repeats!
text:
UPDATE 5/3/21: Got my second shipment today. All in tact! And great color selection!

This is a really great deal. If it is packaged right. The first time, unfortunately the bottles arrived smashed due to poor packaging. This is not the vendors fault as it was shipped by Amazon. I told them about it and they gave me a full refund. I really wish they would take a little more care when shipping. Hopefully my next round will go better.
**UPDATE** 5/3/21: Got my second shipment today. All in tact! And great color selection!
This is my guilty pleasure. It is definitely like gambling. But once every month or so I take the chance and roll the dice. Some times I get colors I do not have and other times I get 4 of the same. But guess what!?! You can mix these colors and get something that you like. That is what I do. I have mixed some shimmery colors with solids and came out with a beautiful shade that didn't exist before! And for around $1 each it is such a great deal!!! Dollar store price but top salon quality. This polish has been discontinued which is really sad because it is my favorite by far. It goes on smooth, love the wide brush and consistency. It helps my nails grow and keeps them healthy and protected. Also it lasts forever. I mean the polish in the bottle will last forever. Like no expiration date. I have some that I have had for over 10 years and they still glide like they were new.

** This is copy of my review from same product but bought different times and vendors***

This a great price for 20 polishes. Buying them in the store would be like $6 or $7. I have to admit this is my favorite guilty pleasure. It is like gambling lol. For around $1 a polish this is such a fun way to try new colors!

I absolutely love this polish. I have used it for years. The nice thing about these polishes is that even after years they still perform as well as the new ones. I love how easy it is to apply. If I am in a hurry, one coat is fine. I like to take my time with my nails so I have steps (below). I like to change my polish every week so chipping isn’t a problem for me. Also I wear gloves when I do dishes or clean to extend the life of my nails.
I love that this polish dries fairly quickly if you don’t put it on too thick. I mean, put on a coat while watching TV or on the PC. Careful not to touch anything! But let it dry thoroughly.
Then if you need a little more tint slap a little thin layer to make it pop. Also here are couple tips I learned through my years of doing my nails:

1. Use the SH Complete Care 7in1 or 170 Clear'd for takeoff as a base coat. (I am pretty sure that is why it’s called that... It makes sense to me) I know they say you don’t need one with this polish, but really it is for easier removal. It makes your life so much easier! Especially with darker colors that seem to get in the creases and under your nail that are a pain to remove.

2. Use a white polish (121 let’s snow for example) under any color to make that color really pop. You don’t need a really thick coat of white either. Just enough to give a canvas for the bright color to pop.

3. This polish is a little thick so mind that when you apply it. Put one streak down the middle of your nail (your first application to each nail) this will be a little thicker then go to each side and touching the outskirts of the middle line to grab some of the excess there to spread to the sides.

4. I use Sally Hansen Salon Manicure Top Coat as well to seal the color in if I am not using a Salon Manicure Glitter Overcoat. I think they both help preserve the color.

4b. I know glitter can be intimidating because it’s such a pain to get off. Don’t let that deter you. If you like the look of glitter go for it! There are polish removers designed for that now. As well as the polish remover in the jar that you put your finger in to remove color. There is one that has little ridges (or brushes) inside the center wall that help strip the glitter. Can’t tell you where or who. But I can say you probably have one in your town.

5. For those of you that like a little shiny nail but not necessarily color. I use the 170 Clear’d for takeoff and add some drops of any sheer color (Arm Candy, Sheer Ecstasy, Sweet Talker, Shell We Dance, Pink Slip) it just looks like a natural nail with clear polish only it hides blemishes! And your nails look super awesome! I get compliments all the time about how healthy my nails look when I do that.

I hope these tips help. These are things I have learned over the years. I love doing nails and I am glad to share the things I have discovered to save someone else some frustration or time.

text: I have never used one of the Dyson hair dryers.  I have heard women LOVE them.  I am not ever going to spend $300 - $400 on a hair dryer.  No way!  This one looks a lot like a Dyson.  I can't imagine how a Dyson would be better than this one.  This one is great!  I LOVE it.  I have curly hair and it works wonderful for me.  I dry my hair upside down with the diffuser on cool.  That really helps to cut down on the frizz.  It takes awhile but it's worth it.  I change out the nozzle and use one of the other tools to dry and lift at my scalp.  It gives me a lot more volume.  The dryer is a little heavier than some of my other dryers.  Yes, I have several.  I also like that you don't get all hot and sweaty when drying your hair.  My husband loves that I don't heat up the bathroom!  I really can't say enough good about this dryer.  It is well worth the price.  If you want hot air, this probably isn't the best dryer for that.  It doesn't get really hot.  The cord is long.  I cut a piece of velcro to wrap around it and it works great to keep it untangled.  Highly recommend!!

title: Not bad minus the lack of volume :(
text:
I needed a new gray wig and after browsing through the same popular brands on Amazon I decided to take a chance and give this wig a shot...regardless of having only one (one star) review. But I’m glad I did though! The other reviewer mentioned that this unit was too thin but if you look at the pictures I posted, I can assure you it’s not. And the color was spot on; It was the perfect amount of gray and silver and platinum that changed shades depending on the different lighting. The part was decent but still needed some extra plucking and some concealer to blend the lace. (I didn’t have it glued down for the photos so you can still see the lace). As for the styling, personally I like more of a beach or body wave so I need to redo the style but I did straighten it out a bit to relax the curls and it took the heat very well.
I only had two complaints: the roots laid sooo flat and the hairline was way too round. The hairline was pre plucked but way too evenly thinned so it still looked kinda fake and the roundness had me pluckin hairs for what seemed like hours lol. I guess I could YouTube some tricks on how to give this girl some volume because now that’s the only thing missing from her!
For the price, you get more than what you paid for...just be ready to spend some quality time giving her some TLC :)

title: Good flosser for the money
text:
DESIGN:

The chrome-plated plastic buttons and cap add a nice touch to the look of the flosser, but it feels a little cheap and will probably wear off quickly. The overall fit and finish is good. It has a nice weight to it—not too heavy or too light. It fits rather comfortably in the hand, even small hands, but the placement of the on/off button creates the sensation that you may unintentionally push it. It doesn’t seem to be a problem, though. The charging port comes with a small rubber insert to protect it from water. It works well, but because it is not attached to the unit, it could easily be lost. It does come with an extra one, though, so that it nice. The portability of this cordless flosser is what I like the most.

PERFORMANCE:

The performance is very good. The power of the water is perfect. One thing I did notice, in comparison to my countertop flosser, is that the third level of power on the cordless flosser is about same as the first level of power on the countertop unit. So, it does not seem quite as powerful as the countertop unit, though the advantage of the portability outweighs the slight loss of power. As for me, I don’t use the highest setting on my countertop unit, anyway, so it is not an issue. A great thing about this flosser is that it is quiet. It doesn’t sound like you are using power tools in your bathroom every time you are flossing. Bottom line, I love this flosser. I think it is a great product for the money. The experience is pleasant, and it does its job well. All in all, a good buy.

title: NO GEL REVIEW
text:
i purchased both the chrome and holographic powders, however i DID NOT use gel nail polish. I know these powders are meant for gel, but i used regular nail polish and the picture polish Revolution glitter base coat and foil top coat.

Starting with the holo, i just could not get the pigment on my nails! i tried it first on my bare nail (with the base coat) and saw the holo tilt, but no actual color. kind of like a holo top coat or something. then i tried it on my nail with regular black polish on the bottom, picture polish revolution on top of that, then the holo powder. unfortunately i got the same result. this way had a few tiny patches where it looked like it would cover the black polish, but for the most part it still just looked like a topcoat.

Now for the chrome. I honestly LOVE the chrome powder. i tried it first on my bare nail with just the base coat, and it made a thin layer of silver chrome, pretty transparent, but it just looked like a sheer shimmer polish. i thought it was really pretty and totally kept it on for about 2 weeks. After that I tried the same black polish with the base coat on top and then powder. The chrome CAME THROUGH. the chrome looks great on top of the black, but i wasnt able to get it to cover my nail completely (had a few patches that were just thinly covered) but i blame that mostly on myself, and not the powder.

overall i 100% recommend the chrome powder. I personally would skip the holographic unless you just want light coverage

title: The best value in the Sonicare line
text:
With Amazon now selling this directly for < $20 this is the absolute best value in the entire Sonicare line. You get the same drive, the same brush head compatibility, 2 min timer and the ability to use your own rechargeable batteries. 5 years ago I purchased a Sonicare Elite 7300; the internal NiCad battery eventually died and the unit is not designed to swap out the battery. I tried, but ended up damaging the rubber molding and had a lot of difficulty finding a comparable battery that didn't require paying a high shipping cost. I eventually purchased the E3000 on a whim at a retail store for nearly twice what it sells for on Amazon nowadays and started using the newer low self discharge NiMH batteries. I get about a month of usage before I have to charge the batteries. And when the batteries finally start losing their capacity -- they haven't yet in 2 years -- I don't have to worry about performing major surgery on my toothbrush.

I'm not sure I understand the water seepage/contacts/failure issues. I own 2 E3000s now, one for travel and one for home, and have used them both for 2+ years now. Neither have failed. I take care not to rinse the base of the unit under running water and I don't leave it sitting in a pool of water or anything. I definitely see the potential for failure as nothing but a plastic cap and a rubber gasket protect the AA batteries from potential moisture, but I think standard, common sense can prevail here. Let me stress -- I do not baby this thing. I use it daily in a bathroom that gets very humid during showers and summer weather (no exhaust fan and no central air conditioning). I clean it every usage under running water and I don't dry it off afterwards. I also have done the same types of things with 2 different E3000 units and have used them for over 2 years now and they have worked perfectly from day one.

Highly recommended. I would actually feel comfortable recommending this over a higher-end Sonicare that was discounted to be within a similar price range as this unit. Being able to use your own rechargeable batteries is simply way too powerful of a feature since that seems to be the limiting factor in keeping this brushes for the long haul.

title: Better than Clarisonic, and more affordable.
text:
This is hands down, the best face brush. Not only is a lot more affordable than the clarisonic, it is a lot softer. I couldn't bring myself to spending $200 on a clarisonic especially when I felt the brush. I have sensitive skin but large pores on my nose. I really was looking for something that was soft but cleaning. I looked into the soft 310,000 bristle brushes, but after reading reviews it would take way too long to dry to be able to use everyday. The Elishacoy brush is super soft but small enough to dry during the night and during the day. I use it in the morning and the night with my cleanser. I only use it for about 10 seconds on each area of my face. Avoid the eyes the skin is very fragile.

PROS:
Soft brush
Two different settings for speed
Fits in hand well
Silicone cover battery to make it waterproof
Ultra-affordable for quality
Made pores appear smaller

CONS:
Korean brand you can't go to store to buy brush replacements

Best wig purchase I've made in a long time (enough to buy twice), and for the price it's an absolute steal! Put it in the cart <3

Read the ingredients, no mineral oil or petrolatum, EXCELLENT!! Most if not all other oil moisturizers have that pore clogging, toxic stuff.

I also have dry skin so I use it on my hands and feet and like it. I got the idea because I have other products marketed for use on hair and body. I CAN'T WAIT to get more of this. At first, I was glad it was on sale and even at that, it is expensive in comparison to other oil moisturizers - but oh, so worth it. I intend to always have this product in my arsenal for my skin and hair, even when it's not on sale.

5/4/2011 update: I still love it. I am african american and now use it on my infant daughter's hair. I do agree though, the sheen seems to wear off after a couple of hours in her natural bush, probably because it is thick and NOT RELAXED. My solution, is that i add a touch of grapeseed or jojoba oil in with it on her hair and so far, so good. Still no build up or dandruff for either of us.

title: Horrible in every way, only for super sensitive skin. Will not give you exfoliation or deep pore cleansing
text: Four years ago I bought a generic version of the Clarisonic called the Nutrasonic. My little Nutrasonic quickly became my favorite four speed facial powerhouse. I loved my Nutrasonic (and still do) but sadly the company has decided to discontinue the brushes so my favorite little machine is no more. Hence I started shopping for a replacement and as before when I bought my Nutrasonic I was dead set on not getting a Clarisonic because of the high price of the machine. I came across the Philips Pure Radiance on Costco’s website and decided to order it thinking I would be happy with it since not only Costco but Amazon has rave reviews for the machine. I got the Philips yesterday and let me tell you this facial brush sucks big time. If you are used the battery operated facial brushes they sell at Wal-Mart, then you may like this weak inadequate machine, but if you are used to a powerhouse cleaner and exfoliater you will absolutely hate this machine as I did. First off, the Philips has only two speeds which are pretty slow. The brushes are super soft (even the supposed stiff pore cleansing brush) and the annoying timer that turns off the machine every 20 seconds to apparently remind stupid people to move to a different spot.  This machine was so weak and truthfully, I hated everything about this cleansing system as it did not leave my skin feeling clean what so ever. If you have super sensitive skin then perhaps this machine will be okay for you but those desiring a pore cleansing will be greatly disappointed. Beyond how weak the machine is in hindsight I should have thought about my current dilemma before ordering the Philips and that being the long term availability of brushes. Since facial cleansing systems are not Philip’s main seller I can guarantee that this machine will be like my Nutrasonic in a few years and have no brushes available. Sadly there is nothing nice I can say about this machine and can only say that if you are used to the Wal-Mart type of facial cleaners and not a Clarisonic then you will probably like this, but otherwise if you are used to the deep cleaning of a quality facial system you will certainly hate this machine. Of course I am taking this one back and have broken down to buying a Clarisonic for two reasons, the power of the machine and the future availability of the brushes. I can’t wait to get my new 4 speed Clarisonic Pro that I ordered off of E-Bay that cost me the same price as this Phillips did but will actually clean my acne prone aging skin.

title: Beard/Stache Facial Hair Growth Supplement Support
text:
Beard/Stache Facial Hair Growth Supplement Support
27 Beard and Mustache Facial Hair Growth Nutrients in 1 Formula. Supports healthy looking hair growth by providing Biotin (5000 mcg) + herbal extracts + vitamins + minerals for maximum beard growth / mustache growth / beard health.

i've only begun taking these supplements a week ago, so i think it is too soon to say what the effect will be for me, but i will come back and update this review in a few months to report what happened. in the meantime, am reviewing the contents of these supplements.

aside from the usual and common Vitamin C, Vitamin E, Vitamin B-6, Pantothenic Acid, Zinc, Selenium, Copper, Manganese, Citrus Bioflavonoid 50% Complex, Goji Berry 40% Extract, Olive Water Extract, and Silica, there are some ingredients i am not very familiar with so i researched them to see what i could learn. here's what i found out:

Biotin Biotin plays a key role in the body. It supports the health of the skin, nerves, digestive tract, metabolism, and cells. Biotin may also help to treat some types of nerve pathology, such as the peripheral neuropathy that can result from kidney failure or diabetes.
Biotin supplements have been studied as a treatment for a number of conditions. In people with type 2 diabetes, early research suggests that a combination of biotin and chromium might improve blood sugar. On its own, biotin might decrease insulin resistance and nerve symptoms related to type 2 diabetes.

Hydrolyzed Collagen Type 1 & 3 are a family of proteins that are part of body tissues, including skin, cartilage and joints. While there are more than a dozen types of collagen, 80 to 90 percent of collagen found in the body consists of types 1, 2 and 3.

L-Methionine Loss of methionine has been linked to senile greying of hair. Its lack leads to a buildup of hydrogen peroxide in hair follicles, a reduction in tyrosinase effectiveness, and a gradual loss of hair color. Methionine is an intermediate in the biosynthesis of cysteine, carnitine, taurine, lecithin, phosphatidylcholine, and other phospholipids. Improper conversion of methionine can lead to atherosclerosis.

Hydrolyzed Keratin is one of a family of fibrous structural proteins. Keratin is the protein that protects epithelial cells from damage or stress. It is the key structural material making up the outer layer of human skin. It is also the key structural component of hair and nails, and it provides the necessary strength for masticatory organs, such as the tongue and the hard palate. Keratin monomers assemble into bundles to form intermediate filaments, which are tough and form strong unmineralized tissues found in reptiles, birds, amphibians, and mammals. The only other biological matter known to approximate the toughness of keratinized tissue is chitin.

Hyaluronic Acid Hyaluronic acid is found in many tissues of the body, such as skin & cartilage. it is well suited to biomedical applications targeting these tissues.

Alpha Lipoid Acid essential for aerobic metabolism. It is also manufactured and is available as a dietary supplement in some countries where it is marketed as an antioxidant. In Japan, LA is marketed primarily as a "weight loss" and "energy" supplement.

Gotu Kola Extract used as a medicinal herb in Ayurvedic medicine, traditional African medicine, and traditional Chinese medicine. It is also known as the Asiatic pennywort or Indian pennywort in English. In traditional herbal medicine, Gotu kola has been used for varicose veins, chronic venous insufficiency, psoriasis, and minor wounds

Grape Seed 95% Extract. Grape seed oil from crushed seeds is used in cosmeceuticals and skincare products for perceived health benefits. Grape seed oil contains tocopherols (vitamin E) and high contents of phytosterols and polyunsaturated fatty acids such as linoleic acid, oleic acid, and alpha-linolenic acid.

MSM MSM is sold as a dietary supplement and marketed with a variety of claims, often in combination with glucosamine and/or chondroitin for helping to treat or prevent osteoarthritis.

Fo-Ti Root Powder F. multiflora is used in traditional Chinese medicine as a tonic and an anti-aging remedy.

Indian Gooseberry Fruit Extract hair oil are traditionally believed to nourish the hair and scalp and prevent premature grey hair.

Bladderwrack Powder. a seaweed found on the coasts of the North Sea, the western Baltic Sea, and the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans, also known by the common names black tang, rockweed, bladder fucus, sea oak, black tany, cut weed, dyers fucus, red fucus, and rock wrack. It was the original source of iodine, discovered in 1811, and was used extensively to treat goitre, a swelling of the thyroid gland related to iodine deficiency.

Nori Yaki edible seaweed species of the red algae genus Pyropia

Wakame Powder New studies conducted at Hokkaido University have found that a compound in wakame known as fucoxanthin can help burn fatty tissue. Studies in mice have shown that fucoxanthin induces expression of the fat-burning protein UCP1 that accumulates in fat tissue around the internal organs. In Oriental medicine it has been used for blood purification, intestinal strength, skin, hair, reproductive organs and menstrual regularity.

Natural Astaxanthin The primary use for humans is as a dietary supplement. Research suggests that, due to astaxanthin's antioxidant activity, it may be beneficial in cardiovascular, immune, inflammatory and neurodegenerative diseases.

as you can see, there's is not much mention of these ingredients as far as hair growth goes. the closest i can tell, some are good for slowing the aging of hair. maybe this translates into darker hair, which makes a beard seem fuller. i am not sure. but.... these ingredients have many other beneficial uses, as you can see, so i figure if my body is benefitting, this is a worthwhile investment, at least for a few months.

title: Great Price, Some Decent Ingredients, but Some Bad Ingredients as Well
text:
I really like products that contain Retinol. They work really well on my oily, acne prone skin. They also work great for combatting fine lines and wrinkles in between Botox injections. Yes, I do like my Botox twice a year.

I like the jar dispenser. It keeps things nice and clean and sanitary and I can control how much product I dispense. This 1.7 oz jar will last a very long time.

I use it at night only for fear of drying my face out, but on rare occasion I have used it in the am and it is a nice cream that glides on smoothly, quickly absorbs and doesn't leave my skin feeling greasy. I am a stickler for that. I won't use a product that makes my face feel greasy, sticky, or tacky.

The amount of Retinol is consistent with that on the market. It also contains Vit B5 and E which are great for repairing damage to the skin and Green Tea.

What I don't like about this product is that while it is 71% organic, it contains Cetyl Alcohol (can dry out the face), Xanthan Gum (a filler) and Phenoxyethanol, which is a very controversial ingredient. It is said to disrupt hormone imbalance amongst other issues. This is very important to me because I am a Breast Cancer Survivor. While I tested negative for hormone receptors and hormone driven cancer, I still proceed with caution. My ob won't even put me on Birth Control because of the hormones. So for me, the ingredients would make me steer away from this product in the future.

title: Close comfy shaves
text:
Norelco consistently ups the ante by continually improving their products.

This is my third Norelco, (my second wet/dry shaver) and by far the best of the bunch.
I've had this shaver for about three years and have never regretted the purchase. It's light, easy to handle, holds a charge for a decent amount of time and provides a nice, comfortable close shave.

One beef I had with my old Norelcos was their inability to get in close under my nose. Not so with this model. It can get in close under my nose and tackle bothersome neck hairs.
Don't feel obligated to run out and purchase more Nivea,[[ASIN:B002GJTYDW Philips Norelco HS803/14 Nivea for Men Shaving Conditioner and Replacement Cartridge (Pack of 3)]] as you can get a terrific shave with a decent shaving cream. Plain old Edge gel works great.

You can spend a ton more dough on a different shaver, by why bother?
And of course, you must make sure to change the heads on a regular basis[[ASIN:B002GJU2YW Philips Norelco HS85 Nivea for Men Shaving Replacement Head]]. Norelco's are notoriously unforgiving if the heads needs replacement: You might as well shave yourself with a damn rock.

If you're a dyed-in-the-wool foil shaver dude and have decided to try out a Norelco, this would be a great model to check out. And just don't try it for a few days and decide it sucks; Be fair and give your beard a chance to get used to it.

title: Beautiful fan brush that performs perfectly!
text:
I was looking for a brush small enough to put just a hint of highlighter on my cheeks, however I didn’t want to pay an arm and a leg for it. Finally, I came upon this little fan brush and decided for the price, I would give it a try.

The brush arrived much sooner than I had anticipated which was a nice surprise. It arrived in perfect condition with all the bristles exactly where they should have been. When I lightly dusted it across my highlighter and brushed it over my cheeks, the brush deposited just the right amount of highlight that I was looking for.

I am very pleased with the results that I am getting with this fan brush. My feeling is that I can always add more rather than try to remove excess. For the price, I highly recommend this brush.

title: Makes my feet tingle
text:
Pumice stone (made in Korea)
It's a regular pumice stone; if you've seen one, you've seen them all. This one fits in the hand nicely and has a pleasant minty scent. I admit I've never found these very useful, and probably won't use it much because they're hard to clean. The one I received is smoother and a different shape from the one in the picture.

Wooden foot massager (made in China)
I used to have a similar one and lost it, so this was one of the reasons I bought the kit. I like this a lot because the ridges are not as deep/pointed as they are on some rollers. It's relaxing without being painful, and feels great on the sides of the feet too. I even like rolling it between my hands. My only complaint is that the wood is a tiny bit rough in places. Not anything I feel when using it, however.

Wooden foot brush (made in China)
I was disappointed in this brush because the one I received has flaws in the wood that make it look grimy. If I were buying this set in a shop, I would have looked through them all and picked one without a damaged brush. However, it's still functional and the bristles are a nice balance between stiff and soft. You can use it to clean your toenails or simply brush it on your feet to get the blood circulating.

Tea tree oil foot scrub (made in the USA)
This is really nice, it has a fine grit that works well without irritating. You get a full size tube and don't use a lot at a time, so this will last a while.

Tea tree oil foot balm (made in the USA)
This is the star of the show, it's heavenly. Smells refreshingly minty, makes your skin soft. Works on any dry skin, not just feet. I couldn't resist using a lot of this, so even though you get a lot, I have a feeling I'll be buying more fairly soon. I really notice the difference right away. I can easily see this becoming a must-have staple.

Bag
It comes in a plastic bag with a green cord drawstring. The plastic is thick enough to make it reusable, though the foot massager sticks out the top. It's nothing special, but sturdy enough to store the set.

Overall
All together, the kit is very relaxing to use after a stressful day. I'm definitely going to check out more of the Earth Therapeutics products that are made in the USA.

The balm and scrub are both vegan, however the brush has boar bristles, so this would NOT be a good gift for a vegan. However, it would be a great unisex gift for just about anyone else, especially people who are on their feet all day.

title: Aloe Vera Gel
text:
This Aloe Vera Gel by Sdara Skincare takes a few minutes to fully absorb into my skin. I worried it would leave behind a sticky feeling, since I could feel it on my arm the whole time it took to dry. But once the gel finally absorbed, I couldn’t feel any residue left behind. I didn’t notice much of any scent either, which is another positive for me.

The gel is clear; the plastic bottle is amber colored to help keep light out. I wish the bottle had a flip top or squeeze lid instead of a screw off cap.

The list of ingredients is what gives me pause. I’m not sure why sodium bicarbonate (baking soda) would need to be added to the organic aloe leaf juice. The product description mentions “we perfected our serum by enriching with Vitamin E, & Citric Acid for improved nutrient absorption.” I don’t see Vitamin E listed in the ingredients.

I will probably end up using this aloe vera gel to make hand sanitizer. The gel is thick, so it should be good for that use.

title: 7110 vs 7310
text: I purchased the 7310 because I haven't changed blades in my 7110 in almost 10 or more years I've had it. 7110 was $30 when new and the 7310 for $25(w/rebate) on 8/27/11. I didn't know it was a 7110 until I shaved 1/2 face with it and the other 1/2 w/7310. The 7110 still shaves well but I was starting to press harder on my face and going over the same area 2-3+ times. When I used the 7310 on 1/2, I went over the 7110 area and could hear, not feel, the blades cutting closer. 7310 has 60 titanium blades vs the 7110/45 ss blades. If I had noticed the model numbers before purchase I wouldn't have bought it. How far off can a razor be in two model #'s? Glad I didn't. Buy the 7310 or better. 5 stars for the 7310, but then again, I don't have a Macho Randy Savage (RIP) beard either.

title: Helped my recurring chalazion and stye problem
text:
I’m writing this review for people like me who struggle with recurring chalazions and styes:
I started getting chalazion 5 years ago on both eyes and I don’t know what even started it. It was very frustrating because these bumps would form on my eyelid and nothing worked on them. I got surgery once to get rid of one on my lower eye lid. They stayed for months and warm compresses and eye massages that doctors recommended me did nothing.
I read many research papers to see what I can do to help my condition and here is a summary:
1. this eye wash helped so much. Since I started using this eye wash two months ago my chalazions are almost gone and very unnoticeable. I wash my eyes every mornings and before going to bed and also whenever it starts to itch.
2. One doctor prescribed an antibiotic called “Doxycycline” I think that also helped my condition
3. I make tea with tea leaves and I let it cool, then I use a cotton ball to put the tea inside my eyes. I use 3-4 drops in each eye.
4. mix of one drop of tea tree oil and 4 drops of olive oil and put it on your eye lid. I wash it after 15 mins.

That’s a summary. Some people say warm compresses help them but for me, I did not see noticeable results. I used to wash my eyes with baby shampoo and it worsened my condition. only this eye wash helped. My condition has improved 80% since I took the steps I mentioned.

title: Great when used daily
text:
I have shaved my head (with a razor) for the last 10+ years. In the last couple of years I've become lazy about it -- letting what hair I have grow out and get sloppy between shaves.

I bought the Conair CleanHead just over a month ago.

The day I got it I put it on to charge and shaved my head with a razor. Starting the next day and every day (but one) thereafter, I've shaved my head with the Conair using no attachments. Each morning I spend a few minutes shaving off the prior day's stubble and I have a nice, smooth head for the rest of the day. BUT if I skip a day (only did it once) the shaving time triples or quadruples.

I have to clean the razor after each use (taking it apart is easy and there's a brush included). I sometimes have to touch up tight spots around my ears but not often. It's great for stubble but sometimes misses longer hairs.

My scalp is smooth and feels good -- I don't have the nicks, scrapes or sensitivity of a razor shave.

I'm (always) on the lookout for something better but, for now, I'm quite happy with this device for a daily, full-head shave.

title: Vivid Comics Color Will Have You Seeing Stars!
text:
(BUDGET MANICURE TIP: Love Amazon though I do, at the moment regular brick-and-mortar stores have started pricing this special-edition line for clearance, making way for the impending influx of holiday merchandise. So, I picked up this set in a store elsewhere for $2.99 today. Now to the actual review...)
Although this polish duo is bright and up to Orly's usual high standards, this is still a basic, high-color product. That factored with the cost earned my 3 stars. Yet there was another polish in this special edition line that I would rate 4 stars in part because adorable yellow stars are included, along with blue polka dots,suspended in a red glitter polish. It's applied regularly, no special effort needed to get the special effects. I normally paint my nails with color only, as this duo provides, with no fancy decorations and bedazzling, and this set is fine for that. Yet I did prefer the red star polish, which Amazon unfortunately seems to have already sold out.It left my nails looking comic-book cool, as if I'd just delivered a Wonder Woman POW! to some evil villain! As a kid watching the series on Saturday-afternoon TV, I wanted to be Wonder Woman when I grew up. Since I'm lucky to carry off the Diana Prince look, these polishes help me channel my inner super heroine and tap some of that energy in a fun way!

title: Super easy to use and interesting design!
text:
I've wanted a good tool that could both straighten and curl my hair and this one is the perfect one for the job (and it was a bonus that it's a great price too)

I'm pretty inexperienced with styling my hair and couldn't be bothered to curl my hair in the mornings with a rod curler that I had purchased before, but this one just makes it sooo much faster in the mornings. Just clamp down on a section, twist 180 degrees, and run it through - even a newbie like me could do this and get super cute loose waves :)

The power button is located on the inside , which is nice so that you won't accidentally turn on the iron by accident as long as it's in the closed/locked position when not in use. Also the temperature control is super interesting because you twist the bottom portion to the right or left to increase or decrease the temperature - so even less buttons to accidentally press while using it 👌

Overall, I definitely recommend it!

title: Made me Break Out a Little
text: I bought this product after seeing a beauty blogger's review about it. Since I have hyper pigmentation from not using sun screen in my youth, I've been trying to find ways to stop or reverse it. I also bought and tried out other products in conjunction with this one as part of a skin care routine, but I noticed that my skin would break out in very small pimples sometimes. After testing and leaving out products to figure out what was making my skin like this, I discovered that when I stopped using this product, I no longer got those small pimples. My skin is somewhat sensitive, so maybe that's one of the reasons, or some ingredient I'm slightly allergic to? I'm not sure. But I wasn't completely surprised as I did notice my face felt just a tad tingly and extra sensitive after using it (even with just one drop, no more than two, each night). During the time I did use it, which was for a few weeks, I have about half the bottle left, I didn't notice my spots lightening or even staying the same. Actually, they got very slightly darker; even though, I used sun screen. I don't necessarily attribute that occurrence to this product though, but certainly I was disappointed that this did nothing for me even after being patient and using it consistently for a while to see its effects. Maybe it'll work for others as the other reviews seem to suggest.

title: No noticeable results after over a month - UPDATED 11/9/14
text:
NOTE: I received a free sample of this product to review.

Like the hair on my head, my eyelashes are thin and fine. Without mascara, they are barely noticeable. I have never before tried an eyelash growth serum and was frankly somewhat skeptical, yet at the same time, I was excited at the thought that this product might be beneficial.

This serum is packaged in a tube that looks like mascara. However, the inner wand ends in a tiny brush that is like a VERY thin paintbrush. I would have preferred a slightly thicker applicator; the brush was SO small that it was difficult to see the serum for precise application.

I followed the instructions for this product, applying nightly along my lash line. I used this product daily for over a month (since late August). Unfortunately, I am not able to discern any noticeable results. I actually took photos, thinking that might show more of a difference, I but do not see any changes in the photos, either. (It looks like Amazon has finally posted my photos, so you can see what you think for yourself.)

The only small improvement I have noticed of late is that AFTER applying my mascara, my lashes seem longer than they normally would with mascara--it's almost as if they are somehow adhering to the mascara better than they would normally. I'm not 100% sure if this is a real effect, but that possible sign combined with the fact that I didn't have any adverse reactions to this product is the reason why I gave it two stars rather than one. Perhaps others will have better luck than I did.

UPDATED 11/9/14: Despite my lack of results after one month, I continued to use this product. It has now been over two months, and I believe that I have seen SOME results. At this point, my lashes DO appear longer--I think that's why my mascara has seemed to be working better, as my lashes are a bit longer than before. However, my results have been nothing like the miracles in the photos posted on the product page--yes, my lashes are a bit longer, but the effect is hardly noticeable to the bare eye (I asked my husband, and he didn't notice). Furthermore, my lashes are neither thicker nor more plentiful; this product gave me a bit of length only, not thickness or any other effect. So, in summary, I upgraded my review from two to three stars, but I don't feel like I can go beyond that at this time.

title: Needed a little alteration, but amazing for the price!
text:
I love synthetic hair because I don't have to worry about the curls losing bounce. This wig is great for the money. Here's what I did to get it lay more natural though. Things you'll need: Verb Ghost Dry Oil, liquid concealer one shade lighter than your skin, powder concealer your correct color, eye shadow brush, mascara in the color of your natural hair, clean and dry mascara brush, scissors and tweezers.

1. First I trimmed the lace to match my hairline. (I cut off a good quarter inch of the front hair with the lace, you don't have to do this but it fits my face better this way).
2. Make the split I like on the side. Tweeze away the space in the middle of the split to make this split more noticable. At the front tweeze a triangle starting point also helps with that more natural split.
3. Put liquid concealer on the underside of the split, use eye shadow brush to brush the powder foundation on the top visible part you plucked. (Any extra powder, just shake the wig gently and it will fall away.)
4. I used the brown mascara to accent the roots of the split and roots along the front of the wig.
5. With wig in hand, spray Verb Ghost Dry Oil on the hair, shake and smooth loose hairs and tangles by hand. Synthetic hair will be soft but not shiny anymore.

Glue in place if wearing all day, otherwise just use hair spray after its tacky to stick the edges down.
(I did not glue it down before taking pics, and I did not blend my makeup into the wig, so please do not judge the edging biased on how seamless. I wanted everyone to see how it looks without these finishing touches.)

title: Bad for Fragrance or Essential Oil Sensitivities - Nice Packaging, though
text:
I have not used this soap due to reasons listed below, and have started the return process. I only can comment on it's scent and packaging.

The Good:
- packaging looks very nice and well presented

The Bad:
- Heavy scent: Definitely has some sort of essential oil(s) in it.
- The ingredient "nervous" cannot be found in a Google search, and an answered question claimed that the ingredient "nervous" was a mistake. That answer is misleading, and made me believe this was free from chemicals and essential oils.

Overall:
If you have fragrance or essential oil sensitivities, you probably do not want to purchase this product, as there is definitely some sort of essential oil(s) in it. As soon as I opened the box, I had a reaction. Not fun. If you are not sensitive to fragrances or essential oils, this is probably okay. I am giving 3 stars because the product is well packaged and looks nice, however, I cannot give more stars due to the misleading information in the product description and answered questions.

title: Earth Therapeutics versus Generic Sleep Mask
text:
I bought this Jan. 2010 and have been using it on and off. This item does come with a very strong fragrance that is very nasty. As other reviewers have said, you will definitely have to get rid of the fragrance before you can use it. Secondly, I have several generic sleep masks from American Airlines that will be used as a comparison.

The Earth Therapeutics sleep mask keep slipping off my face as I twist and turn. It is some what padded which I find unnecessary since I don't like any pressure on my cornea at all. The only way I could keep this from slipping off was to tighten the strap, which caused some pressure on my eyes due to it being padded.

The American Airlines branded sleeping masks are made of cotton and never slip off. They are not padded but I don't want them to be. They cover my eyes without putting any pressure on my cornea.

In conclusion, the Earth Therapeutics are not any better than generic sleep masks given to you on airlines. If you travel and need some sleeping masks, just get the ones that your airlines offer.

title: love this product
text: This is my first review, ever. I have used this Aloe for the past year and I was going to reorder it again for the forth time, when I noticed the lack of good reviews for the product, this made me feel bad because I just love it. First time I bought it because I had some irritation around my eyes and I wanted to buy something without alcohol so it does not get irritated more. I started using it around the eyes then my whole face. I love how my skin looks in the morning, very clear  and glowing. I had some sun damage on my forehead and all of a sudden it started fading then it disappeared completely. I started putting it on my forearms and hands and it also helped with the sun damage that you get on the arms from driving. Now I use it on my chest, full face and forearms and my skin looks amazing. I only put it at night in place of a night cream, it is a little sticky for a minute but it is absorbed very quickly. By the time I finish putting it on my face it has already dried on my chest and forearms. You have to be persistent and apply it every night and you have to be patient. It seems that the time it takes to fade the discoloration has to do with how bad the skin is damaged. I had areas that cleared faster then others. Also it seems that some areas might look worse before they get better. It will look darker then as if a little more visible before it completely disappear. I have sent this to my sister overseas and she also loves it. You do not need to use a lot, it spreads very nicely. I recommend this product for anyone who has sun damage and discoloration on the skin, just be patient and consistent.

title: Natural, organic, vegan & revitalizing retinol serum! Rejuvenates the skin :)
text:
After having an amazing experience with Retseliney's moisturizer, I was thrilled to test this retinol serum during a one time promotional period. Reseliney uses all natural and certified organic ingredients, which I absolutely love! The serum arrived in a white bottle with a pump which works very well to dispense the right amount on serum unto my fingers for perfect application. The bottle is labeled with directions for use, product ingredients and a small description of the product. This review is written in my own words.

The serum has a transparent and liquid texture and a very mild earthy scent. I have been using this serum every morning after I wash my face and I am addicted to the tightening feel. My face is very sensitive and oily and I am so pleased at how wonderful this retinol serum works to keep my face looking vibrant without breakouts or irritation. This serum contains natural antioxidants and vitamins that my face require to stay firm and wrinkle free. Retseliney uses only organic, natural and vegan ingredients to insure that the retinol serum is top quality and chemical free for better results. I absolutely love this serum and use it during my morning routine. My face looks bright and radiant and I can feel the glow!

The serum is small enough to fit in my carry on bag and fit in my purse with my cosmetics. I am very satisfied with this serum and will be purchasing another bottle once I run out. I would highly recommend this product to my friends and family! Retseliney has helped my skin rejuvenate and feel alive with their high quality retinol serum and all natural ingredients.

title: Effective Wipes (with just one) - Recommended for those with Sensitive Skin!
text:
Biore Cleansing oil make-up removing clothes come in a plastic box of 44 and are made in Japan. At first, my wife thought that these are small and thin, but she found that one was sufficient to remove makeup and mascara, with mascara top coat (which can be challenging to remove with other wipes). My wife has tried several brands before, including top-rated wipes, and all of them except for this one irritates her skin slightly. These do not! Her skin is pretty tough, and she doesn't usually have reactions to cosmetics.

There is a label on the inner package with Japanese writing, that's not translated into English on the package. It says to make sure to use the plastic box to store the wipes to ensure quality and peel off the sticker completely; don't remove the wipes from the bag; make sure to close the lid after use. I'd suggest that the manufacturer add a translated version on the package. The package says to use the entire package within 45 days after opening, so if you don't wear make-up every day, you may not finish the package before they start to dry out.

My wife loves these! Highly recommended, especially for those who have sensitive skin!

title: Leaves feet noticeably soft for weeks
text:
I love this type of peeling foot masks. It's so satisfying to peel off large swaths of skin and be left with softer feet for weeks. I've used masks from a few brands, and this is a pretty solid one.

Unlike some other brands, this mask starts having noticeable effects on the very first day after application. I noticed my foot was itchy on the side and didn't think much of it until the afternoon, when I looked down and realized it was itchy because the skin was already beginning to dry out and peel.

Unfortunately, this mask doesn't work like the others to dry out large pieces of skin that you can just peel off. Instead, small tiny bits of skin start peeling, as if you were chapped in various different spots. I found that it was most effective to sit down after a shower, which helped loosen the dead skin, and carefully rub or pull at the dry bits. Only then larger areas start coming off, though still in small bits at a time.

However, this mask leaves your feet softer for weeks! Of course, the first week after is the softest, but even three weeks later, my feet are noticeably softer than before. I'm honestly tempted to use this mask once every 6 weeks just to maintain the softness.
