title: A fair diffuser
text:
I've been diffusing essential oils for almost 30 years, ever since I purchased my first diffuser from Whole Foods back in the 1980s. This diffuser is not representative of the latest generation of diffusers, but it fits nicely within the last generation. The latest generation of diffusers are not waterless, cordless (rechargeable) and portable. I have both types of diffusers in my home and much prefer the latest gen.

This is a nice diffuser but is slightly more expensive than it should be, even with the ceramic appointments. Like all water diffusers you put water in the chamber with a few drops of oil and let it go. The light is nice but doesn't serve much purpose other than to remind you that the unit is on when you cannot see the cold steam. I'm not a fan of the whole thing being lit because it does cause me to take a second look if my eyes slightly open while I'm sleeping. I prefer a thin band of light. Of course on this unit you can turn the light off.

It doesn't come with any oil which is actually good. I find that when an oil is included in with a diffuser it's often the cheapest brand around and smells awful. It might be enough to turn a newbie off of diffusing if they don't know that high quality oils exist. I recommend Plant Therapy.

I don't buy into the claim that this can be used as a humidifier because the output is just not strong enough. After shut-off there is always water still left in the chamber. This is not a good thing because if you want to change oils, you have to diffuse water with vinegar for a couple of run-throughs and then wipe down the inside. Of course there is nothing wrong with left over oil if you don't mind a blend, but sometimes I want one pure smell for a specific purpose.

title: Fantastic!
text:
This 6-head Suttik replaces what seems like an identical motor version with 5-heads. This breed of shavers is the first I've ever used in the steamy hot AM shower every day. Day after day. What a time saver! It does a fantastic job. It delivers a better shave than my really high-$ screen shaver.

6 heads is an improvement over 5. First, it simply covers more area. This is your head ---> a spheroid with protuberances. The 6-head shaver conforms to stuff like your ears and jawline better. 6-heads get the cutter closer to the nooks and crannies where those obnoxious thickets of long hairs might survive for a couple of days before someone points and say, "Ewee!"

The shaver is small and easy to hold. A charge lasts more than a month. Cleaning is easy. I use shaving cream to lubricate, just like a regular razor. A 'dry' shave in the shower might sound like an oxymoron, but you'll know what I mean when you use it once.

I'm sold. Adds at least 5-minutes of goof-off time in my morning routine. I'll take every minute I can gather. Perseverance might payoff with a straight razor shave, but the time saved here to goof off in the AM pays off immediately!

It's revolutionary.

Was my review helpful? If so, click "Helpful" so I can know.

title: quiet and convenient
text:
This razor is my second norelco rotary, my first is a 5100 series about 20 years old. Compared to that, this razor is much cleaner, quieter, and more comfortable. The new razor doesn't *sound* like it turns the blades as fast or as powerfully as my corded dinosaur, but it seems to me that it cuts more effectively, which is what I hoped for.

Cleaning this new razor is a breeze - just a quick rinse under hot tap water and I'm done. Having cleaned it this way, I can't imagine paying for the extra cleaning station and chemicals - I note with some interest that the printed user instructions and website FAQ also recommend the hot tap water cleaning method...

Comparing my shave to a triple-blade disposable system I use, the electric doesn't cut quite as close - I can't tell immediately after shaving (my face feels smoothly shaved in either case), but I notice stubble somewhat earlier with the electric razor.

I shave about once a day - maybe 5-10 minutes or so. So far, the battery life is about a month as advertised. Very convenient indeed.

title: Wow! I Love it!
text: Wow! What's not to like about this watch? I've owned a stainless Submariner, a stainless and gold GMT Master, a stainless Datejust, and a stainless AirKing, all of which I gave to my sons 16 years ago when I retired. I've been wearing a Swiss Army "submariner homage" since then. It finally started stopping for no reason, so I decided to get something new instead of getting it fixed. I researched my next watch purchase for about a month because I wanted a watch that I loved to look at, and would last me until I die. (I'm 72) I certainly love the way it looks, and I plan to wear it until it until one of us dies. If I outlive it, I'll buy a new Rhonda 515 movement for about $20, install it, and do the same thing all over again.. Phoibos has taken the watch market by storm since it started a few years ago. The reviews are great, and now I see why. When Phoibos took their name off the dial, and replaced it with the octopus logo, that was a stroke of genius. If you're trying to make people think you're wearing a Rolex, buy a Rolex or a clone. If you don't give a crap what people think, want a great quality watch, and love quartz watches as much as I, you won't find one anywhere with this kind of quality in the $120 price range. I bought a blue one, and as soon as they start making the green again, I'll order one of those. I love it!

title: BARBER CHAIR IS OK
text:
It all started with receiving the box in terrible condition about 7 weeks ago with no protection. I was surprised that almost everything was there. It was missing a center bolt mounted under seat cushion used to maintain the seat assembly solidly on the hydraulic stand. They did not supply the bolt, nor the instructions and they had the audacity to tell me the bolt is not required. Without that bolt, if chair is lifted just 2 inches , it will fall off the base. Went to local Lowes and purchase a bolt and washer.

There were damage problems with the calf rest and the rubber trim at bottom of base which took them 6 weeks to replace and when I finally received them they were damaged in different areas. This company does not take pride in their workmanship nor their quality control. I will not contact company again with their current philosophy that they have on their products.

The Chair itself looks good and works. It lifts approximately 6 to 8 inches and also reclines which is a plus. Some areas of the material is very thin (low quality) and therefore is prone to damage from the smallest abrasion, so precaution is needed when moving chair around. It turns easily after foot rest is lifted using hydraulic lever. Seat and back cushion is comfortable but tight. Its an ok chair all around , but I would probably try a different brand next time around depending on price and presumed quality.

title: This thing is absolute garbage.
text:
I don't like writing bad reviews, but I felt like this thing rates one. I really can't return it for a refund because it is what they said it is and it does what it says it does. It just does it really poorly. The different pads are a nice touch, but they're useless if the motor doesn't spin them effectively.

I put 4 brand new AA batteries in it, and got in the shower. I selected a pad, turned it on and it spins at probably 160 RPM - between 2 and 3 revolutions each second. Which isn't bad, except that it slows way down if you put the motor under a load (such as pressing it to your skin). It then bogs down so badly that I could easily cover the same area on my body with a 99¢ scrubbie and do a better job.

So this is going straight into a yard sale. Save your money and buy a scrubbie and a back brush - at least they don't eat batteries.

title: Nice Nintendo Switch case for normal use
text:
Overall a good case for the Nintendo Switch. First off, there is no bad smell! The Switch fits great in the bottom half of the case, there are indents for the Joycon bumps and elastic straps that are found on most other cases also. The elastic straps work to keep the Switch from sliding out of the zipper is undone, but it's also difficult to put in and awkward to remove since there is no good way to pull the device up. The screen is covered by a padded and soft divider that has slots for up to eight game cartridges. The top half of the case has a loosely netted area that could easily hold cables and earbuds. If you have a smaller charger it could probably fit also. An extra set of Joycon controllers will fit, but is tight.
The exterior is not black, but more of a grey with a logo at the right top side. A bonus is the included hand strap.

title: Not only makes clean up easy, it also gives me better food.
text:
If you haven't gotten a silicone baking mat yet, and you use your oven AT ALL, you should really get this!

It's incredibly easy to clean up. It doesn't matter whether it's those cookies that always stick to your favorite cookie sheet or a gooey cheesy mess, it really does simply wipe off.

Because it's so easy to clean, I was also able to get some of the best parts of one of my old standby recipes to come out. I like wrapping thin cut chicken breast around some kind of cheese and other fillings and baking it. The problem with this is that some of the cheese inevitably leaks out. Even on glass cookware, this is wasted food that I just can't get back in an edible form. With this mat? Nice crispy melted cheese fried in the oils that came out of the chicken breast. One of the tastiest parts of the dish, and a new favorite!

Cleaning up the dish above? I cooked it on a 1/4 sheet raised lip cookie sheet, with no prep other than putting the baking mat down. After pulling the food out, all it took to clean was an easy job wiping the mat off and then just basic washing to both the mat and the pan. While some of the juices from the chicken did get under the mat, NONE of the solids did and it was just simple to do.

The only downsize to this mat that I can find is it's size. I want a bigger one, because I don't want to keep using cooking sprays and parchment paper. It's perfectly sized for a 1/4 sheet baking sheet, but that's really what I consider a 'small' sheet unless I'm in the RV where my oven will only barely even fit this mat.

I received this product at a discount in exchange for my unbiased review.
I've been looking for reasons to use the oven for this

title: Big mistake - try Sebastian's cellophanes treatment instead!
text:
This seller sent me the brunette color glaze instead of the clear one so I guess this review is about the colored glaze - I'm wondering if the clear one would have done a better job! I was mad enough that I got the wrong product, but nothing could have prepared me for the result! :(

The brunette color glaze version was a disaster - made my hair terribly dry and tangled!. I followed the instructions to a tee, so I'm really wondering how smtg like this can still be so heavily marketed when it can actually damage your hair that much.

After that devastating experience, I did some research to try and find a glaze that would actually IMPROVE the appearance of my hair instead of worsening it (!) and tried the Sebastian's cellophane hair treatment - omg that was a life-saver!!

I was absolutely thrilled to see that that product actually DOES coat your hair and gives it a silky feel and look.

The only thing is having to sit for 15 min with the hairdryer in your hand - but this is the only product that has returned my hair to a healthier look so to me, it's still worth the pain and the price - around 25$ per tube.

I will be trying the Sebastian tinted version next - fingers crossed!

title: You get what you pay for
text:
Buyer beware!
Pros:
Packaging was great. Nothing spilled out or burst open. Colors are gorgeous.

Shipping was ok. I got a message basically saying shipping was delayed due to my error of not providing the right address, yet I ordered 4 items the same day and provided the same shipping address. They supposedly corrected the error and got it shipped to me in less than a week.

Now, let's talk about the cons:
I can't say this product is a true polygel. When using the slip solution or isopropyl alcohol, the solution dissipated and the brush dried out within seconds. Once I was able to start smoothing the product out, I got one good pat before the product became extremely sticky and unmanageable due to the brush drying out. It definitely took some finesse to get it to work. Also, do not use the dual forms as the product is not thick enough to hold it's own without moving under the light. If you have paper forms or nail tips, you will have much better luck with this product. I will say it definitely took me way too long to do one set but filing the product was great and so easy.

After trial and error, if you want to take the time to make it thicker, put some on a smooth flat surface and add clear acrylic. Make sure to use a metal spatula to mix the 2 products if you so choose to. Also, THIS PRODUCT IS VERY STICKY. If you are not careful, you will get this product everywhere.

I would give more stars if the polygel was a thicker constancy and the brush didn't dry out seconds after dipping into the slip solution or isopropyl alcohol.

I will give this kit a try again when it's time for me to change my set, but I'm not sure I will purchase this kit again.

Okay so enough about the front cover of the palette lets get to the size so it measures 4 by 6 in a half inches so just the perfect size to fit in an overnight bag or even an everyday purse. I mean thats perfect for me because i am always wanting to have the choice of choosing a different color but i mean you can only fit so many eyeshadows in your purse so this palette has been a perfect fit for me i now have the perfect palette that i can always have on the go. Okay so the shades in this palette i mean the pigmentation is definetly there i mean of course there are some colors in the palette that aint bright enough for me but thats just a personal thing because i love dramatic looks. If you are a beginner this is a perfect palette for you i mean you have so many colors to choose from and this is such an amazing bargain the price is very affordable. Okay well now that i wrote a novel about this boardwalk eyeshadow palette i will get to my final opnion and of course i definetly reccommend this eyeshadow palette.

title: Nice look; feels pretty solid; but average in performance & value
text:
This is a “dressed up” version of the Wahl “LifeProof” shaver (which I also reviewed). It has a more upscale look and feel to it, plus adds a battery gauge to show the charge level. However, the shave quality is the same – OK, but not a standout.

The case feels more solid than the LifeProof and, while it’s more of a traditional look, I think they did a nice job with the overall aesthetics. It is pretty grippy even with wet hands. It does not, however, feel as comfortable to hold as the more sculpted models such as most of the Panasonic shavers and some Braun shavers.

The shave quality of this model is identical to that of the LifeProof: very Remington in how it feels (close, but rather aggressive). Performance is best on the easy spots (cheek) but not as good on the more trickier spots (neck and jawline). The flex head helps with dealing with face contours but it lacks enough travel to do an excellent quality job.

The aggressiveness of the shave is dialed down when using it wet. It needs a very, very thin layer of shave cream (the gel style creams work well) or the head gets bogged down.

Love the trimmer! The positioning could be improved to make it easier to use, but it cuts very cleanly on sideburns and ‘shash trimming.

The controls are clean, easy to read and tell you all you need to know (charge and battery status).

I like the feel of this better than the Wahl LifeProof, but the shave quality keeps this at the same rating (average, which is 3 stars). It’s a very workable shaver, but for the price there are better choices available from Panasonic (anything in their Arc line) or, for less money, from Remington.

title: High-Quality Synthetic Hair Extension! Will Buy Again!
text:
I was immediately enchanted with this hairpiece from the second I tried it on. I've tried wearing it a few days in a row now, and I'm not tired of it yet. First, I'll start by saying that I ordered it as an add-on item in "dark blue to peacock green." With Prime, it only took two days to get to my house; very fast and convenient shipping, and the hairpiece was encased in a clear plastic bag; I threw that away, as I keep my extensions in a silk hair case. This hairpiece is actually pretty high quality for synthetic, eccentric hair, so I would suggest something like that to keep it safe and untangled when you're not wearing it.

In my opinion, the product images do not really do this hairpiece justice. I can't say anything for other colors yet, but the dark blue to peacock green is pretty true to the product images, with sapphire blue roots and aqua-blue tips (although I will say the hairpiece I received is not half-and-half blue and green like the picture; mine is like an ombre gradient, approximately 2/3 blue and 1/3 aqua tips).

Many of the reviews here also have mixed comments on the thickness of the hairpiece; maybe the people who say it is "thin" are comparing it to their own natural hair? I will say that this piece is very thick for a single-weft hair extension. A weft is like a curtain of hair with a seam holding it all together at the top. I wear extensions almost every day after a butchered haircut took 12 inches of my hair. Authentic human hair extensions come in wefts of varying widths, usually one clip wide or four clips wide, and the hair is very thin because the wefts are meant to be layered on in sections, like natural hair. This blue hair extension is 5 clips wide--wide enough to fasten on your head from ear to ear. Because of my experiences with authentic human hair extensions, I was expecting this blue piece to be thin and underwhelming, but I was wrong and am very happy about that.

I also have an asymmetrical haircut that is close-cropped on one side, so this extension is actually too wide for me to wear across my nape (as the model does in the product image), but I am still able to use it. I curve it to clip from my left nape to above my right ear and it suits my needs and still looks real, despite its unusual color.

Since there is so much hair on a single weft, as other reviewers have stated, this hair extension is a bit heavy. That may take some getting used to, as improper clipping could cause some discomfort and irritation from pulling on the roots. However, once you get practice putting it in, you will learn the best way to clip it without any tugging or pulling of the hair.

It is synthetic material, and while I noticed that mine was shiny, it was not like "Barbie hair," as some reviewers said. I did not need to rub any baby powder in; I just wore it to work for a few hours a day and the shine eventually dulled to look a little less like synthetic material. I will try to add some photos to this page of what the extension looks like in my (straight black) hair. I also noticed that it became easily tangled after a few days of wear. This is not really a problem; I am afraid to brush it too often though, as it sheds (which is normal, but I want it to last).

Lastly, despite being synthetic material, I have tried straightening it on the high setting on my Conair flatiron with success. You can also use a razor or scissors to make any modifications to the style or length, if necessary.

Overall, I think you will be very satisfied with this product if you buy it. I liked it so much that I placed an order for dark pink/light pink and yellow/lime green. These were not add-on items, however, so I will be waiting until mid-August to September to receive these other hair extensions. Hopefully they will be as nice as the blue/peacock green, because I was not disappointed!

title: Has artificial fragrance, smells amazing
text:
TLDR: GET THIS mask if you know you dont react to fragrance and simply like the smell of honey. DO NOT get this mask if you are looking for a mask with lots of honey and no added fragrance ingredients.

My skin loves honey. I have therefore tried many honey masks in pursuit of one that my face loves the most. I almost returned this face mask because when I recieved it, one of them seemed to have leaked through the plastic package they were bundled in. However, after deciding that my face needed honey and it wasn't really worth it to return it, I decided to give these guys a go.

Let me first say that this mask is the best smelling mask I have tried yet. I cant believe how lovely it smells, it took me back a bit. This concerned me of course as when something smells amazing it usually has fragrance added to it. When looking at the ingredients, I found that it does have "fragrance" in the ingredients, though it is farther down on the list of ingredients so it is not very much.

Near the bottom too, however, is propolis. Which is the only ingredient related to honey in this mask. Which was rather disappointing as this means this mask has hardly any honey in it.

I probably wont reorder this unless my skin somehow loves it.

title: This is the best razor for me
text:
Everyone's experience with a razor is different. I've tried a lot of different types, and I was more than happy to let go of my Gillette Fusion Power early last year. I bought a Shick Hydro Power. I thought it was great, cheap all around with an amazing cut. I even have the highest rated review on the product on Amazon currently. Now, I looked at the reviews for this saying Hydro 5 is better. It might be better for one person or another, but I have used both extensively and like this one better.

I have been using the Proglide POWER (yes, power) version for about 6-7 months now. I just purchased this manual version to take with me to basic training (in case the power version would be confiscated). I'll backtrack and talk about my experience with the Hydro 5. When I used it at first, it was kind of rough. I figured it would get a little better and it did, but it still was uncomfortable. I fooled myself into liking it because of its price and how long the lubrication strip lasted. I was getting only a week on my Fusion Power before it started to become uncomfortable because the strip faded out, and I was getting 3-4 weeks with the Hydro 5 until it ran out. The lubrication on that thing is funny though, because sometimes the whole thing will fall out of the razor.

My experience with the Proglide has been positive in every way as soon as I took it out of the box. I have learned long ago to not hype up any product until I've used it for a long time. With 6-7 months of experience I'm telling everyone how it is. The proglide can cut through thick stubble as good as it does on your 5 o clock shadow. I hadn't shaved for about a week and used the Proglide and it cut me down smooth with no pain and with ease. I bought myself a replacement pack of 8 cartridges and I have run through 6. That means in 6 months, I have used 6. Actually 7 if you include the one it came with. For those of you out there who know how to make a razor last, one of these can go up to a month or two. For beginners or average shavers, you will get at least two weeks worth before you even consider changing it, and, the lubrication will still be there.

One thing I hated about the Hydro 5 was the grip. It was a horrible design. With the Proglide, it was made with you in mind. It just feels natural holding it, and every stroke feels just right. As we all know, replacing Gillette cartridges can be a pain in the neck (not physically). To buy a pack of replacements, you're looking at about $30 for a pack of 8. The same will cost you under $20 with the Hydro 5. Not everyone cares to pay for it, but it's more than worth it. It's the best shave I ever had in my life, and I don't mind paying $10 extra every 6-9 months for a better experience.

Think of razors like gas, and you are the car. You only require regular gas, but if you decide to go with the premium (Proglide), your car will run faster and be more responsive.

I will state again I have not used the manual proglide yet, but it's the same thing without vibration (which I don't use much anyway).

Hopefully your experience is as good as mine.

Here is a run down of stats in my own opinion for the Proglide.

Fusion Proglide
Standard: $9.74 (comes with 2 cartridges)- replacement pack of 8 - $28.99
Power: $10.94 (1 cartridge) - replacement pack of 8 - $31.99

Comfort level: 5/5
Effectiveness of each stroke: 4.5/5 (sometimes you have to run the blade back over)
Smoothness of shave: 5/5 - Your experience may be different, but this is the best shave of my life
Cartridge life: 4/5 - Depends on how you use it. Will get you 2 weeks minimum and up to 2 months maximum
Design: 5/5 - The thing I hate about most razors is the design. This one feels great in hand

Overall: 4.8/5 - It's not perfect, but the best razor I've ever used in my life

It comes out of box with a razor holder which most gillette razors have, but others don't. Also, I strongly recommend buying the Proglide shaving cream. it's the best I ever used in my life. You can literally see your hairs rising in seconds if you look closely.

Good luck everyone!

title: Proven ingredients, but a poor value
text:
I’m not all that impressed with these products. Neither container lists the ingredients on the label, instead providing the ingredient list on a card that’s in the box. It shows that the cleaner is a 2% salicylic acid wash, which is a pretty tried and true formulation for treating acne. It is a low-lather, but still effective cleaner, but I didn’t experience any difference with this compared with other 2% salicylic cleaners that cost far less. It’s a fine cleaner, but way overpriced for such a tiny (0.51 oz) container.

The repairing night cream is a combination of retinol (0.05%), niacinamide (3%), and hyaluronic acid (2%). All are proven performers, but again, it’s quite overpriced considering how low of a percentage of retinol is in the formulation. It does a decent job of moisturizing and did not cause any skin irritation. I am a fan of retinol, but the concentration in this cream (0.05%) is well below what I usually use (either 0.1% or 0.5%).

While this product combo is on the right track, the value is just not there considering the amount in the container (one-half an ounce for each). While I would certainly recommend a salicylic acid wash and a retinol-based cream, there are many other options that provide these options at a far lower cost. Four stars for performance; 1 star for value; overall rating of 2.5 stars.

title: I love snails!
text:
I love snail slime. I have been reading about it for a while and am so glad I pulled the trigger to purchase it. I get occasional acne and scar easily. After two uses I saw a profound improvement in my skin. Nothing works that fast.I am a believer and am buying up all the slime I can find. The use of snail slime to improve skin dates back to ancient Greece, where Hippocrates, the famous physician, reportedly prescribed crushed snails and sour milk as a way to rid the skin of inflammation. In more recent times, the use of snail creams started when Chilean farmers who handled snails for the French market noticed their skin was visibly smoother. Nowadays, it is the secreted slime discovered by these farmers that is harvested to produce snail cream, not crushed up snails.
How the Slime Works
When snails are agitated, they excrete a thick fluid as a means to protect themselves. It may sound odd to use something like that on your skin, but remember that humans and most other animals also excrete oils as a way to protect their skin. In fact, the oil your body excretes to moisturize your skin is made up of lipids, sebum oil, and dead skin cells. As a snail protects itself, the slime that is excreted from its body is packed with nutrients like hyaluronic acid, glycoprotein enzymes, antimicrobial and copper peptides, and proteoglycans. All of these nutrients are already commonly added to beauty products and are thought to provide many benefits to skin.
The science behind snail slime as a viable beauty cream is mostly derived from lab studies where the effects of the slime have been tested on a variety of cell cultures. In these studies, research suggests that snail slime will stimulate the production of elastin and collagen, increase fibronectin protein production, and stimulate increased proliferation of fibroblasts. While all of these traits could be beneficial for skin, there have yet to be significant cosmetic trials or research into how snail slime actually affects the skin, and how long those effects last. In fact, there haven’t been any significant clinical trials on how a specific beauty product containing snail slime actually works on this skin – just the slime itself.
One of the main difficulties in conducting clinical trials on the benefits of snail slime is that the market has not fully figured out how to guarantee the results of testing. After all, if snail creams are to be effective, then the slime has to have a consistent concentration of the various chemical compounds, but this is difficult to guarantee. Because the success of trials leans heavily on the quality of snail extract used, the concentration of slime and the way it is excreted and processed, test results can sometimes be misleading. Some companies that manufacture snail creams do choose to keep a steady supply of their own snails so they can better regulate the extraction and formulation of the slime.

I was given the opportunity to purchase this product at a discounted rate in order to test and give an unbiased review. As a longtime customer of Amazon, I also depend on others' honest reviews for purchases. The discounted rate of a product will not have influence over my personal opinion. I purchase lots of items through Amazon, some I get for free, some I get at a discounted rate, and some I pay full price. I will always disclose if I receive an item for free or a discounted rate. I do a lot of reviews so here is a break down on how I determine my star rating of a product:
5 stars – I love it! Is awesome and I highly recommend everyone to purchase this product!
4 stars – I like it, but there are some flaws. Don't we all have flaws? I would purchase again.
3 stars - It was okay, but I would probably not buy it again unless there are no other options.
2 stars – There may be worse products out there, but you can definitely find better choices.
1 star – I dislike the product, I wish I had not purchased it and recommend others to not purchase.

title: Powerful and light, but tough to reach the inside spots!
text:
It’s compact, light, easy to charge and the tip isn’t too pointy. Well it’s pointy but it’s not a hook so I initially was afraid but as I used it, I realized it wasn’t going to stab my gums.

When I turned it on, I was surprised at how powerful the little tip was sonicating. I found it easy to use on the outside of my teeth, but it was awkward to get to the inside of my teeth. I think I will have to practice holding it differently, maybe. I bought this because I build up tartar on the inside of my bottom front teeth—directly where my tongue rests. I did mh best to clean there but saw only mild results after one use.

I will use it a couple more times and see if maybe I see better results and hopefully I will get used to how to hold it so I can access the inside of my teeth.

Now if someone did this for me, I think this little tool would work wonders!! It seems quite powerful. I will keep trying and update my return if I get better results.

title: Practice makes perfect
text:
I also had never used a clamp-less iron before, but I have spent a lot of time trying to achieve that nice loose spiral that holds but doesn't look too rigid. This really does the trick. I have long,straight, baby fine hair and summer humidity is pretty much death to anything I try, and the curl falls right out. Somehow this holds up really well.

My complaints with it are just that even though it does get really, really hot, it seems to take longer to heat up than some of the "instant heat" irons I've been used to. So even though it doesn't take too much time once it's fully ready, there is a longer wait time on the front end, and not really a clear way of telling when it's fully ready either--it doesn't have a color-change light or dot like some of them do. Also, the glove is kind of dumb. I took a stab at it the first few times. It's weirdly small, for one thing--so it's awkward putting it on, you can still feel a good amount of heat through it, so it's not that effective at sparing your hand, and I could never get the hang of using it with the glove. It just felt really clumsy and took at least twice as long, so I stopped using it, and it's totally doable. You do have to be a little careful, sure--but honestly, I was hand wrapping my clamp curling iron almost the same way when I was trying to fake the spiral curl look so this isn't all that different---only this time it works. Now that I've had it for about a week, I feel like I have it really down. Since it takes longer, I can't use it every day during the morning rush, but it's kind of a fun way to shake things up.

title: Awesome!
text: Ok, I wanted to wait before I did a review on this product because I literally just got my labret done and it's not even healed. But the ball fell off of my starter jewelry and fell down the drain in my shower and these pieces were the only ones I had to replace it with unless I wanted to let it close up. I will admit it was hard to get on, the studs are very dainty and I kept dropping them while trying to align them with the post while I held it against my lip. I'm not sure how much of that tho was because my piercing wasn't ready to change jewelry and it was still slightly swollen. Well, after I got it in I haven't taken it out and am letting my piercing heal with this piece of jewelry. I paid attention to the back of my lip, the sides of the post are a bit sharp and for the first day or two I had begun to worry that the acrylic post was doing more than just 'nesting'. I was worried it was cutting the back of my lip. Ladies and gents, this fear has subsided and I am having a totally normal reaction to this jewelry. After a week I decided I absolutely LOVED these pieces because the post doesn't feel like it's eroding my enamel or my gums, and I think you literally have to be pulling on your jewelry and twisting it aggressively for it to really tear up the back of your lip. After my swelling has gone down I've realized the posts for these pieces are short enough for me to practically never bite them when I'm chewing and long enough for my lip to not feel in any kind of danger. The studs are sturdy and I really don't think they'll fall out. Only thing I'm sad about is that I lost the clear one in my haste to put another piece in my unhealed piercing. But like I said, I think it was only hard to get on because mine was new and it was quite painful to change it. But tbh my piercing has been healing completely fine with these in it!

title: Either You Love it... or you hate it....
text:
I can see why this shampoo gets either 5 stars or 1 star from its reviewers — and almost nothing in between.

PROS: I like it because it contains only healthy ingredients with no SLS or other detergents nor any added fragrances — a blend with these features is such a rare thing, not easy to find. Also, it's very mild, with no stripping action, and leaves my hair soft + easy to comb even without conditioner. It's also one of the few shampoos that does not make my scalp itch. So, it's become my product of choice!

CONS: Often (depending on how dirty the hair is) it doesn't suds on the first application... which is not what we're used to and can be frustrating... so, you could end up pouring on way too much of it toward that end. But just be patient: Don't add more, just rinse and do a second application, if needed... it will suds nicely.

I first used this shampoo years ago and was glad to rediscover it a year or so ago via Amazon. I am definitely a fan.

title: Works well overall - but "bristles" vary in length year to year, become brittle and break off too easily.
text:
It is difficult to find a large barreled curling iron/brush. HELEN OF TROY offers a broad range of assorted sizes, and they work well. This is my third or fourth purchase of this same model in about 10 years. Even though I purchase the same model, I find that the hard plastic "bristles" vary in height from year to year. The short "bristles" sometimes place the hot barrel too close to my skin, especially when the "bristles" break off (which is fairly easy and common, possibly due to overexposure to heat). The models that arrived with longer "bristles" are more suited for longer hair, however they sometimes "tangle" in the hair -- and THEY, too break off all too easily. (I have recommended to the manufacturer that they add something slightly more flexible to the "bristles" to keep them from getting brittle and breaking, or perhaps design the barrels so that new sets of bristles can be installed, however -- why would they take my suggestion when consumers can simply replace the entire unit with a new one?) The heating elements in these units have never failed; only the "bristles" are problematic.

Overall, I like this model, but I find that I need to replace it after about 12-18 months of use due to loss of "bristles".

Go get some of this and thank you instanatural for such an amazing customer service.

title: refreshing, hydrating, basic, budget friendly, add to self care themed gift
text:
Good basic sheet mask that feels cooling and hydrating. Feels nice when my rosacea has made my face feel warm (I have visible veins/redness from rosacea; no other issues). Excellent for use in the bathtub, or for a mini nap, or simply to help relaxation. Might even be perfect post skin treatment sessions that may leave skin feeling irritated.

Stocking stuffer!! I love how budget friendly this pack is. I love making little self-care packages to hand out, and while I will buy and use masks in the $5-35 range, I prefer the $1-3 masks for the token give away (think: facial mask, hand cream, lip balm, etc.. tiny cheap self care packages). I also prefer the cheapies only because I am not sure if the recipient will definitely use it, and the higher end masks tend to be more discerning in what it attends to. The more basic masks tend to be good for nearly everyone, and the resulting feeling is at least a fresh, hydrated face. So these are a repurchase for that purpose, as well as for my own use on days I want to keep it simple.

I love that it has lots of moisture. The serum can be patted in all over face, neck, chest, even elbows & hands after.

For all that it is, I give it 5 stars for this level of facial mask.

title: This stuff is the gold standard
text:
I’ve been using versa products for years, as that’s what’s typically used in standalone self tanning units in my area.

Maybe just because I’m so obituuated, I don’t mind the characteristics smell, and this formulation is really thick and rich, but still quite easy to spread. It also definitely changes your color in a very pronounced way. This one is not a gradual or faint gradation. It will absolutely transfer onto your clothes, because it has instant bronzing agents in it. Sometimes that’s pretty useful to see if you’re getting streaks— you can work to address it as you go. But I don’t love the instant bronzers because they always, always transfer to my clothing. This one, like all of them, will do that, particularly if you sweat.

I guess self tanning products have really vaulted up in price, but at the current listed price of 44.95, this does seem expensive, though it is generously sized and works really well. If you like the idea but want to save money, maybe look into the newer tanning drops. By getting the darkest ones, you can just add a couple to your lotion to get an incremental tan because a little goes a long way. But if you like the appearance of a tan while you’re waiting for the pigment to appear over the course of a day or night, this one will give you the longer-term results along with the instant gratification

title: LIFE CHANGING LIP BALM
text:
An avid amazon user & review reader, I have never felt compelled to write a review until now---
As an Eczema sufferer, I have had chronic dry lips as early as I can remember. They are always peeling, chapped and dry no matter what I put on them. And I have tried just EVERY PRODUCT there is including: lucas paw paw (worked but basically just petroleum jelly), burts bees, carmex, eos, eucerin (worked somewhat well), blistex, vaseline and even prescription strength steroid ointment for my lips (that worked but i didn't want to keep using them since they are unhealthy). This product is hands down, THE BEST lip care for my peeling lips. My lips stay moisturized and the peeling is gone!! Its amazing and I will never go without again. Buy this, you won't regret.

I did buy this at an Aveda salan where is was only $8.50, however.

title: If you are head shaver do not buy!!! You will be immensely disappointed!
text:
This is the worst shaving cream I have ever seen!!! If I could give it 0 stars I would!

I've been shaving my head since 1996. I've experienced a plethora of shaving creams, however this is by far the worst one I have ever seen.

First off, it has almost zero lubrication. It literally felt no different than me trying to shave my head by with just water. It's thin and runny and ran down my head and face like regular liquid. This formula has almost zero lubrication. I had to use several handfuls to shave my head because it kept running down my face. It literally felt like using just white water with no lubricant whatsoever.

I finally had to use some bar soap just to get enough lubrication to finish shaving my head.

I don't know why they changed formula, but they need to go back to research and development on this one!!!

title: Pretty nice, for the price!
text:
I wasn't sure what kind of quality to expect when I ordered these blushes, but let me give you some key things I love and hate about them. Overall however, I think it's a good value for what you pay for!

Pros:
- The price, obviously is extremely affordable
- The color range makes these palettes very versatile for many skin tones
- Nicely Pigmented
- Sleek and portable palette design

The Cons:
- The palette is made out of very cheap plastic. Opening it for the first time, I was worried it would snap. It may not be good to travel with.
- No matte shades! Every single blush contains shimmer, although some are more subtle than others
- A bit chalky in formulation, but it deposits well onto the skin using a brush
- Not very long-wearing. Maybe 2-3 hours before the color began to fade

title: First natural deodorant that has worked for me!
text: I've been using this deodorant exclusively since I received it last week. So far I have found the unscented works best. I have sensitive skin, but have had no negative reaction to the scented ones. I've had my husband smell me at the end of the day to see if I stink, and while there has been slight B.O. from one armpit or the other when using the scented ones, the unscented one, when making sure to have full coverage of pit area worked great. Be careful when putting on dark clothes as I've noticed it leaves deodorant type white marks. Currently my armpits are hairy and like I said, works well. All in all I would say 5 stars. I love that they have this trial pack, and it works better than any natural deodorant I have tried! The scented ones don't smell bad, but are kinda hippiesque so I think mixed with perspiration they tend to smell more like an oil trying to cover up a BO scent. Totes worth a try to see how your body respond to it chemically-we are all so different, it takes a while to find one that works for you. UPDATE: I ended up ordering the full size version of the unscented. Still working great after a few weeks. No negative reactions, no stink. I sometimes have to rub it in with my hand a bit as it gets stuck a bit in the hair, but no big deal really. LOVE THIS STUFF.

title: Affordable and pretty but not my favorite
text:
The "VITU Jojoba Lipstick Makeup with Vitamin and Antioxidants for Long Lasting and MatteTones 0.07 oz", is a very affordable lipstick. I like affordable products so much because if I really like them, I can purchase them over and over and not worry about breaking the bank. I did have a few issues with this lipstick though, it doesn't go on smoothly...you have to press harder than a typical lipstick to get the color on your lips.

It has jojoba in it but I found it drying when I used it and it doesn't have added scent or taste but it does have that lipstick taste and scent to it (it reminds me of lipstick my mom used in the late 80's that I would play with). It did not make my lips look juicy like the picture on the product page either, it's more matte and it has mica added to it so that it sparkles a little. It does transfer and it does wear off but it leaves a stain on your lips which is typical in red colors. Overall, I think it's not bad but not my favorite.

title: Great temporary tattoos!  Not just for kids anymore!
text:
I love this company's products! This is it my first purchase of temporary tattoos from this company and so I am familiar with their brands and products. I would definitely recommend their products if you're looking for high-quality tattoos that are modern and stylish.

These are super easy to put on and tends to stay for several days versus some of the other tattoos on the market. The one I have in the picture below has been on for four days now and it still looks just like the day I applied at.

I love all of the different packs of variety so that you can get a pack of that suits your style and needs. I would definitely recommend these. Application is really simple. You pick the tattoo you want and place it in side down on your skin wherever you would like it to be. Just make sure it is straight and where you want it before you apply the wet paper towel. You just take a wet paper towel and place it over the paper side of the tattoo. The other side you adhere to your skin. Then hold it there for 30 seconds, and when you left it off, the tattoo stays on your skin and the paper peels away. Then, just let it dry.

Love these! I highly recommend them. Happy shopping

title: Smaller Than I Thought But Still Beautiful Head Pieces🥰
text:
Hi, It’s Glamorous Gem, hope you all are having a wonderful day,

Anyway, I always like a universal accessory that goes with just about anything chic and simple. This would definitely complete any outfit with those attributes.

I love the design. The silky texture is super cute and attractive, and the pearls really make these headbands pop. So elegant, smooth, and stylish.

These are also quite thick. There is silky fabric on the outside and thicker fabric on the inside surrounded by a metal band. There’s a single band on one side and three metal bars on the other lined with fabrics and pearls as well.

This doesn’t look or feel too cheap. They are above hair store quality and seem very sturdy. They fit on my medium sized head quite easily and with no slip. They also don’t seem super uncomfortable when wearing as well.

The only problem I had with these are that they are a bit smaller than I expected. I thought they would be wider to cover flyaways, but these are a bit slimmer than expected. But, they do seem cool and unique. And, these are perfect for those that like to show their edges with a fresh new hairstyle like me.

So, all in all, these fit my recommendation for switching it up in spring. It’s always good to try something new.

It disperses easily from the pump bottle and feels cool as you apply it under your eyes. It blends in quickly and leaves no residue so it won’t interfere with applying makeup. I’ve had trouble with other eye gels that make my eyes water but not with Beneleaf.

title: Works great, so little cleanup
text:
I've owned a few trimmers over the few years, and none had a built in vacuum. After losing the power cord to my last one, I went shopping and found this gem. The vacuum intrigued me -- would it really be worth the extra $20-25 over a standard trimmer? One word: Yes.

I'd always put off shaving my beard due to the necessity of cleaning up afterwards. I just trimmed my beard, which had grown out over the past two weeks, and amazingly, the sink below me only had a few pieces of hair in it. That is totally acceptable in my book, and with this I'll be keeping my beard trimmed often because cleanup isn't a hassle anymore.

Highly recommended. I can't speak for longevity, except that the trimmer I'm replacing was a Norelco that I bought almost two years ago, and it's still running (albeit it just died because I can't charge it anymore).

title: Not impressed with the colors and package was interesting
text:
I'll start by saying the shipping was impressively fast compared to the expectation that was set, so that was a nice suprise! But then I opened up my USPS box to find 4 packages of 'China Glaze' branded boxes with the Essie polish inside. I won't dwell on that too much though because they were safely packaged and all arrived in perfect condition. I just felt like the inconsistent branding was a little curious and makes me question the seller.

Ok from here I was off to a descent start color wise when I saw the first one was a nice shade of pink. Then there was another pink.. and yet another. Each only varying ever so slightly, not enough to warrant all 3 in one shipment. Next I find a bright orange, black, navy blue, gold sparkle, bright blue, another blue, and wine sparkle. This is clearly a lot of polishes no one wants and is really a "you get what you pay for" situation. I am very disappointed at this because most reviews said that was not the case. What a bummer... stuck with a lot of ugly polishes I will never use.. except maybe 1? In summary, I pretty much just paid $30ish for Qty.1 Essie polish. title: Good for clarifying but questionable ingredients
text:
I have color treated hair that I've been dying for about 8 years now and I have to really maintain it to keep it healthy and looking smooth. I learned some time ago how much better my hair looks when I use Argan Oil treatments and would do them once a week or so to make my hair super soft and pretty. I started using shampoos and conditioners with Argan Oil when I saw how well my hair responded to it. Some brands left my hair feeling heavy and oily so I really wanted to find a good clarifying shampoo. I started with just a little bit so I could gauge how it would work for me but once I lathered it up in my hair I realized how much a little bit can cover. I don't need nearly as much as I do of most other brands. I washed it that night and followed it up with my regular conditioner, another Argan product. My hair felt really clean. I have a lot of hair and at the top I couldnt feel the heaviness and resistance I had been from over moisturizing. The smell was ok, I like something that is stronger and prettier, this one just kind of smelled like soap, and it faded quickly after It was dry. As long as my conditioner smells good I don't mind it too much but if you're planning to use it alone you might notice. I think it worked well for getting it clean without leaving it dry or feeling like straw. I don't feel like my hair is as soft as with most Argan Oil shampoos, at all, but I think it'll be a good one to use intermittently to clear the buildup from all of my oil rich products.

I was surprised and disappointed at the ingredients list when I looked. From a company called InstaNatural that boasts this as a holistic treatment I didn't expect to see so many chemicals. I would have preferred more familiar ingredients, and natural, obviously. Argan oil isn't even listed until the second to last ingredient. I like how it worked but I'm not sure how hard it would be on my hair if I used it regularly.

I did receive my shampoo at a promotional price for testing and evaluation purposes. I was not paid for my review and was asked only to provide whatever feedback I may have about the product, whether it be positive or negative. I welcome both positive or negative feedback as well. My review is solely based on my experience and honest opinions of this shampoo.

title: Snozzle with Xtava Black Orchid and Conair Infinity Pro
text:
Similar to some of the other reviewers experiences, I find that the SnozzlePro works pretty well for me, but I do have to be very careful and make frequent adjustments -- it sort of exploits design problems with the tools you are using. I used it with a Conair Infinity ProPro and an Xtava Black Orchid.

Pros:
-It definitely fits my Conair Infinity Pro and probably won't have any trouble fitting other hair dryers.
-It's thick enough to be sturdy, but thin enough that I can stretch it onto the hair dryer.
- I don't experience any leaking or weird smells.
-I can use it (carefully) with the Xtava.

Cons:
-It is made of rubber, so when it heats up the rubber becomes more pliable and loose around the diffuser. I would recommend using this with cold air if possible. Unfortunately, my Conair Infinity Pro has a setting for "Cool" that isn't cold enough. So, I find that this works best if I keep my finger on the blue "Cold Shot" button the whole time.
-The Xtava has a very short connection point, so even though the SnozzlePro works pretty well, there's not a ton of space for it to hold on. That's a problem with the Xtava though, not the SnozzlePro. In particular, it seems to detach most at the top of the connection point and it will fall off while I'm using it. I just sort of use it gently and keep an eye on that top part to make sure it isn't slipping out.

Overall, I think the SnozzlePro is a great product that would probably work best with a diffuser with a longer connection point than the Xtava and potentially a hair dryer that has a truly cold setting.

text: IFirst of all, I have to admit that I didn't use this product as the instructions suggested. According to the instructions, you are supposed to leave the product on your skin for about 5 minutes, waiting for it to dry before you rinse it. I just used it in the shower, rubbing it onto my legs. It basically dissolved and rubbed into the skin, so there was no reason to rinse it off. Afterwards, I shaved my legs, and they felt noticeably softer. I normally have pretty dry skin, particularly on my legs. The product left my legs feeling silky and moisturized (which surprises me considering it's a salt-based scrub). It also smells really wonderful, without having a super overpowering scent. A little went a long way with this product as well. It's also nice to note that there aren't a thousand ingredients in this that I don't know how to pronounce, just a few gentle ingredients. I'd say it's definitely worth the money, especially if you're looking to soften up your skin for the summer.

title: Longer Lashes, but Not Thicker or Denser
text:
I used this Butecare Serum for 2 months to write this review.

Things I liked:
- I noticed longer lashes and eyebrows. It is especially noticeable when I apply mascara because my eyelashes are naturally light-colored.
- The applicator brush is soft and easy to use
- I still have plenty left after using for 2 months
- I did not have some of the issues other reviewers have mentioned (burning, allergies, etc.)
- Not very expensive

Things I didn't care for:
- I did not notice a change in density of lashes or thickness of existing lashes
- The serum is watery, so I had to gently massage it in after applying, otherwise it moved like tears on my eyelids
- I woke up in the mornings with sand-like dried particles in the corners of my eyes, which I don't normally experience when I am not using this product

title: Beware the Bottle
text:
The only reason I did not give this product five stars is because of the packaging. The cap comes off in two parts for some reason. One part opens the dropper tip and the other opens the entire top of the bottle. When I went to fill the makeup cup of my airbrush I uncapped the wrong part of the Aeroblend bottle and before I realized it I'd literally dumped $24.50 worth of product down the drain... and on the vanity, and on the floor... :(

I used the makeup once before accidentally pouring the rest of the bottle out. But, from my very limited experience, Aeroblend is an amazing product. It evenly covered my very uneven skin and made it look almost flawless. Aeroblend blended perfectly with my actual skin tone (although maybe I just got lucky and chose the right shade on the first try) so it looks completely natural. I'd been using Art of Air makeup and had to do a lot of mixing and experimenting with different shades every time I put it on. Even then I could never get it just right. Aeroblend is very light (not heavy) so it didn't look or feel caked on my skin. It didn't even feel like I was wearing makeup. I definitely recommend this product, just be sure to pay attention when you're opening the bottle.

text: I'm new to nail polish and this was the first time I EVER did my nails with gel polish. I have had professional applications but never do I paint my own nails, like on a rare occasion I'd use clear polish or a light glitter so believe me I'm brand new to all this nail stuff. This powder is AMAZING and so beautiful. I found it helpful to cure the top coat for half the time, apply the powder more saturated at the nail tip and rub upward to the cuticle with the applicator tip to help buff the powder. A little powder goes a long way and next time I will apply MUCH less powder, too much powder gives a metallic look. I had a more sheer halo look which Ioved but I messed up the other hand and had apply more powder to even the look out lol. My nails didn't come out perfect because I'm very inexperienced in nail care but I'm really happy with the results and will continue to practice. I highly recommend this powder. I used gellen gel polish romantic color set, gellen base and top coat and the sunuv 48 watt smart lamp 2.0 and this powder.  I had sucess with all these products. I was hesitant to try the powder after spend so much time with the gel polish but I'm really glad I played with it. I'll will definitely order this again. Just try it, it's fun. text: I started using this after being prescribed 2 prescriptions for Rosacea...1 of them costing me $142 (Ivermectin). I didn't see great results with either Rx. I got smart and researched the cause of Rosacea. These demodex mites that your skin is allergic to causes Rosacea. I have been using this facial wash for about 2 weeks now. I notices a BIG difference within 2 days. I also noticed my face didn't have these little biting sensations, which I get on my scalp and arms. I used it on my arms and the biting went away. I have used this in conjunction with OVANTE SHAMPOO. I used the shampoo 2x/day for 5 days and man has it done a wonderful job. The smell isn't that great but I don't care...it's not terrible and it's obviously killing the mites. Be careful to not use any other moisturizers because it clogs your pores. I only use moisturizer on my neck, chin and forehead now.  I have also just started using a Tea Tree Oil body wash. It seems to help. I can't believe my doctor never mentioned this stuff....it works better than the Rx for me. I know not 1 thing works for everyone but I was getting Rosacea postules and redness on nose and cheeks so bad. Not anymore...it has been cleared up.  I do watch what triggers my rosacea...chocolate and alcohol. But using this facial wash has helped so I can have a little of chocolate and alcohol without having terrible redness the next morning. Y'all PLEASE try this stuff. It's worth it.

This masque is so gentle, that I also use it on my eyelids and around my eyes. It's natural, so it doesn't irritate that sensitive skin, but gives both my eyes and face the moisture it needs.

Would definitely recommend--especially to people with dry skin and acne. It's a great thing to add into your regime, and even though you may not feel it tingling or making your skin super tight (which is how I typically knew my past masques were 'working'), it really is effective.

And lastly, this is a steal! There is so much product in the container, and you only need about the size of a quarter every time you use it. Definitely get your money's worth.

title: great for people and pets
text:
This set of scrubby brushes with silicone points is a cheap but effective way at getting your scalp clean from all the products and dead skin that build up on it every day. Compared to the similar version that has hard plastic point, this style is much kinder to your skin while working just was effectively. The first time you use one of these to help wash your hair you will notice a difference in how much cleaner your scalp and hair feels. These little scrubbies are also fanatics for giving pets a bath because the points get down through their fur and help get their skin clean. They also help do a better job than just spraying water to get all the product out of your pet's fur while rinsing them off. I highly recommend using a hair catcher for your tub drain as these will also help your pet shedding process.

Besides washing hair, these also work well for head massages if you have a headache (help increase blood flow), massaging in and distributing any serums/oils/tinctures you may be applying to your scalp to heal with hair growth, relaxing and gentle back massage (us lotion or oil so the points move easily across skin), grooming your cat or dog, or giving a gentle hip and leg massage to your pup if they are older and have arthritis. Heck, you can even use one for planting seeds - gently pack the dirt a little, level it flat, then use one of these to create perfect holes to place seeds in. Kids can also use these to paint - dip in washable paint and make giant spirographs. Oooh, they also work to gently itch things like hives or an allergic rash when you can't use something sharp like fingernails, and these can be easily disinfected.

The included shower cap is okay. The outside is a light blue satiny material with a white edging that reminds me of the old fashioned sleep caps worn a centuries ago, like the grandma in "Little Red Riding Hood." The plastic lining is too loud and crinkly to be worn while sleeping. It's fine if used for a head covering while doing a deep conditioning treatment and it's easy to rinse clean. If you were to turn it inside out to use to keep your hair from getting wet, it would work fine for a bath but not for a shower. The white edging material is fabric and is on both sides of the cap, so that at least would get wet and possibly allow water to soak up through the rest of the fabric. Still, it's a nice little freebie that the seller includes.

text:
This is the first time that I've used a mask from this line. And I have to admit that the packaging influenced my purchase:)
I really like the feel of the serum and the light fragrance. I, unlike other reviewers, couldn't fit it over my ears. Also the eye holes were way too small. Tip: use the peeI off backing and laid it on my neck and chest.
Left it on for 10 minutes and when I removed it I have small bumps all over my face, but now after 30 minutes, they seem to be going away. Once the serum dries it does feel a little tight on your face but it does not dry out the skin. I finished with a rich moisturizer to "seal the deal." The next morning my face felt very soft but still had a few bumps around the mouth area. My advice would be for those who have dry, sensitive skin, might want to be weary of this mask. title: Hair feels like silk afterwards.  Very pleased.  But, I'll need a second treatment.
text:
I wish I'd taken pictures. As soon as I put this on my hair, I could see the difference between this and other products. I have colored my brown VERY fine, VERY thick split-personality hair (curly under, straight on top) for years ranging from a bright burgundy red to black, to various browns. This last time, I had used a bleaching prep product to add a few vibrant blue streaks. But all I got was black that won't go away.

SMELL

I've tried bleach and non-bleach products. Those smelled terrible. This didn't have much smell in comparison. It definitely didn't smell like the other reviews.

COMPARISON TO OTHER PRODUCTS

The other products I tried destroyed my hair leaving it crunchy and damaged. They also only managed to lighten the color from the crown and left the near black almost exactly as I started. (Beware of the competitor non-bleach product that says it only takes 20 minutes. The box doesn't say you have to rinse it a MINIMUM of 20 minutes. That one also required a recolor, which stabilized the hair. I think my hair would have snapped off had I not recolored it.)

COST EFFECTIVE

My hair is so thick, I had to use 2 boxes of other products just to get coverage. This product went on so easy, my hair sucked it in and softened as it went on. I only used 2/3 of the solutions in this product. I'm impressed that I didn't need it all. (Hairdressers always charged more to color my hair because it's so thick.)

PROCEDURE/OUTCOME

I left it on for probably 40 minutes, used a cap, but didn't heat. The top lightened to my natural light brown color (I forgot what that looked like!) and the black is now a rich brown, not even really a two-tone, which I've had since my experiment. But, it still needs another treatment to get it the rest of the way. My hair has blond overtones now, not brassy at all.

BONUS!

My hair is really, really soft. I would do this as a conditioning treatment! I am ordering a second batch to retreat since what I have left isn't enough. Next time, I will heat my hair slightly to see if it will take it the rest of the way. Anything left over will be used for a future treatment to condition my hair. (I live next to the ocean and the salt and wind has been really hard on my hair so I'm very happy to find something to help.)
